programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark goes undercover as a patient in a residential home to solve the murder of a retired policeman. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve investigates the alleged suicide of a US marine, but has to deal with a female NIS agent who is determined to place the case under military jurisdiction. Barry Van Dyke stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is mugged by three young thugs, but his passive reaction to the incident arouses the suspicion of a local reporter

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry and Della travel behind the Iron Curtain to defend a woman accused of killing an East German agent during negotiations to evacuate her granddaughter

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker tries to prove the innocence of a boy with learning difficulties who has been accused of murder

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella trail a killer around Brooklyn while investigating the murder of a man whose body was dumped on a subway track. Their only lead is a woman seen in a home movie in the victim's flat. Danny looks into the death of a gallery owner with links to the Mob, and the corpse of a building labourer with a foul-smelling wound poses problems for Aiden and Flack. Guest starring Kelly Hu

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A young Navy Seaman is found dead, with his eyes gouged out, after being involved in a car accident. Director Vance makes a rare return to field duty, pairing up with Gibbs to investigate when it becomes clear the killing seems to be connected to the case of Kip Klugman, a former NCIS agent he once locked up for stealing evidence. Starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam goes undercover as a bomb specialist when he investigates the death of a demolition expert, killed while driving the car of a notorious club owner. However, he faces a race against time to find explosives located across LA. Meanwhile, Callen learns more about his past and Hunter decides to reshuffle her team

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble hitches a ride with a local clergyman, but finds himself in jeopardy when the cleric is killed by a sniper's bullet

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry deals with industrial espionage while investigating the murder of a private eye working undercover at a laboratory to identify a corporate spy

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A childhood friend of Gibbs' dead daughter is stalked by a marine who recently returned from Iraq, and asks the agent for help. Memories of his murdered family lead him to make the case a priority, but he acts on his initiative and leaves HQ without his badge, making DiNozzo fear he is risking his job for a personal vendetta. Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly star

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker's river-rafting holiday becomes dangerous when one of the group is murdered

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the deaths of a radio DJ and a naval officer who were both killed during a live broadcast, only to discover there are a number of potential suspects. Evidence leads the agents to suspect the involvement of a radical group called Military at Home, prompting Tony to investigate an upmarket gated community - where his door-to-door inquiries yield an enticing proposition or two from the female residents. American drama, starring Michael Weatherly, with Nadia Bjorlin

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a reporter is murdered as she prepares to break a story about a mysterious Libyan freedom fighter named El-Libi. The activist is wanted by the country's government after establishing 'radio free Libya' in the aftermath of Gaddafi's closure of the internet. Meanwhile, more details of Callen's past begin to emerge

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A taxi driver and an admiral working at the Pentagon are found executed by the side of a road, but the agents have trouble ascertaining a motive - until they uncover links to a woman searching for her missing husband, a political refugee from Africa. Meanwhile, McGee makes a discovery about his new girlfriend. Starring Sean Murray, Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: When a mob hitman is killed by a car bomb after being acquitted for the third time, Mark Sloan strives to prove a calculating female lawyer is responsible. Gina Hecht guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. A convicted wife-killer takes Dr Sloan hostage, but Steve fears trigger-happy police officers may be the real threat to his father's life. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on medical leave, Ducky reopens a 12-year-old case by talking officials at a cemetery into releasing the body of a navy commander whose post-mortem he carried out 12 years previously. Meanwhile, the team is called in to investigate the death of a sergeant from carbon monoxide poisoning - and finds a copy of Ducky's exhumation request on the man's laptop. Crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. First lieutenant Michael Torres gets involved in a fight before falling to the ground, and is found beaten to death the next morning. The only witness is his friend and colleague Captain Joe Westcott, who suffers from post-traumatic stress disorder and has trouble distinguishing reality from his past military experiences in the Middle East

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs's father is increasingly frustrated at being kept in his son's house under armed guard, and Franks joins his security detail after emerging from the convent where he had been hiding out. Meanwhile, Ducky finds evidence at a helicopter crash that gives the team an important lead in tracking down Paloma Reynosa, and the drug kingpin's brother Alejandro may have an ulterior motive when he tries to secure a deal involving her extradition. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to locate a kidnapped girl but has to contend with her grandfather, a former member of a disbanded special forces unit. The agents discover he has been secretly negotiating with the kidnapper, who was once in his undercover team and has decided to kill off his old colleagues in a bid to atone for their actions. Meanwhile, Abby is forced to work with an intern, and makes him follow her idiosyncratic rules. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the home of a bomb disposal expert who shot and killed an intruder during a robbery. The case appears to be straightforward until Tony discovers she lied about being alone during the incident, and the evidence suggests a link to a case in which a man walked free after paralysing a member of the public in a shootout. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team becomes involved in an international incident when the body of an American petty officer is found by an estate agent in a bungalow that has been repossessed. An indentation on the victim's skull leads the agents to believe he was killed on a British ship, but collecting evidence from the vessel proves challenging. Guest starring Tracy Middendorf, Daniel Gillies and Kristen Ariza

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve strikes up a friendship with an attractive blonde, unaware she is a serial killer with a penchant for policemen. Chat show host Sally Jessy Raphael guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An exhausted Jesse faces a professional dilemma and finds himself forced to contradict one of Mark's friends to ensure justice is done. Medical detective drama, starring Charlie Schlatter and Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs fights for his life on board a US Navy hospital ship after being shot. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and Joanna Teague head for Shanghai, where they hope to take down the leaders of the terrorist group known as The Calling. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs throws himself back into his work following his near-death experience, updating his appearance and tackling a new risky case alongside DEA agent Luis Mitchell, who wants help bringing down a drugs cartel. Concerned about his colleague's apparent enthusiasm to re-enter the field, DiNozzo discovers Mitchell is the son of a former NCIS agent who gave his life trying to save Gibbs' late wife and daughter, and this attachment is causing Gibbs to break his own rule against personal involvement in a case. However, a significant danger is posed by Benson Long, the cartel's head, and Mitchell's top target

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is found murdered in Quantico, mere hours after calling Gibbs to discuss a possible case. In response, Bishop and McGee go undercover as a married couple to run surveillance on a lieutenant and his wife, who may be connected to the death. Starring Mark Harmon, Emily Wickersham and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Maggie tries to hide her anguish as she tells her children they have the chance to go to Malaysia for a year with their father. New receptionist Alice packs her bags after the four-week trial, doubtful she will be asked to stay on, and Nina catches Ellen off guard after trying to put on a brave face

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. Emma and Sally are first to respond when two massive explosions rock Sun Hill. John and Gina monitor the shocking scenes of carnage from the station, while Callum takes charge on the ground, directing his team to deal with multiple casualties, confused and hysterical survivors and petty thieves taking advantage of the chaos. When it becomes clear a pervert has been posing as a doctor to molest injured women, Callum loses his grip and vents his fury on the man, but his troubles have only just begun

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small

programme.title: Lovejoy

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Emma asks her parents to babysit while she and Peter attend a function in London. Meanwhile, Hester delivers meals on wheels to one of Sonia's old ladies but gets lost, collides with a policeman and causes a traffic jam. comedy, starring Julia MaKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: New resident Daisy tries to instil some discipline, but Diana is worried about her intentions toward Tom. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Alcohol threatens to prevent Yorkshire's first trainee astronaut from doing his job, so Foggy prepares to step into his boots. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth goes to North Yorkshire to meet her long-lost cousin Kate, and ends up investigating a case of demonic visitations. Amiable Geoffrey joins her in an attempt to provide moral support - and is soon drawn into his own little mystery

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Gene investigates the shooting of a Ugandan Asian man and jumps to the conclusion that the attack is drug-related when he finds a suspicious bag of powder on the victim. When the substance turns out to be heroin, the policeman becomes determined to keep it off the streets of Manchester - and decides to do whatever it takes to get his way. Fantasy drama, starring Philip Glenister and John Simm

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A skeleton uncovered in the foundations of a swimming pool is identified as a young lottery winner named Cheryl, who was reported missing 17 years ago. The prime suspects are her fellow quiz team-mates, who won their lucky windfall as part of a syndicate, and while some have put their money to good use, the victim's boyfriend has fallen on hard times and the media remain convinced he is the key to her murder. As allegations of affairs, fights and betrayal begin to surface, the team is left to ponder whether money really is the root of all evil

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The accidental fatal shooting of a Polish teenager in an armed police operation sparks a series of murders, which links Turkish gangsters, a grieving family and a fractured firearms unit. As the inquest delivers a lawful killings verdict, IPCC investigator Vicky Sharpe calls on the Lyell team to investigate the connection between the events and review the police evidence, but Sharpe's relationship with the family of the teenager and her suspicion of the firearms division lead Nikki and Thomas to distrust her motives. Feature-length episode, starring Emilia Fox

programme.title: Cadfael

Drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Terence Hardiman, Michael Culver, Ian McNeice, Christien Anholt and Julian Glover

programme.title: Where the Heart Is

Meanwhile, Anna and Beth look after a pensioner they believe should be transferred to sheltered accommodation _ but he's none too pleased with the proposal, and promptly escapes. Drama, with Leslie Ash, Luke de Woolfson, Philip Middlemiss and guest star Leslie Phillips

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Heartbroken that Karen has left Skelthwaite to take care of her heroin-addicted son in London, a dejected David yearns to leave the village, taking Jess with him - but soon discovers his daughter has made a happy life with new boyfriend Joe. Meanwhile, Sally catches truant schoolboy Nathan in a compromising situation with Candida, and Anna fears for the health of elderly farm owner Essie Crayford. Rural drama, starring Philip Middlemiss, Kelly Wenham and Danny Seward

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: A line-dancing champion whisks Anna off her feet and takes her out to dinner _ but the course of true love doesn't run smooth and she's soon pining to be rescued from the predicament. Beth is delighted to discover she's having a baby, but her joy is short-lived when it emerges the pregnancy is ectopic. Nathan is heartbroken when Candida reveals she plans to tour the world during her gap year and Rita confesses she suspects her husband Martin is having an affair. Kerrie Taylor, Lesley Dunlop and Josie Lawrence star

And as the unrest grows, he's forced to decide where his true loyalties lie. Feature-length adventure, starring Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Abigail Cruttenden, and Caroline Langrishen

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet

Drama, starring Tim Healy, Timothy Spall, Kevin Whately, Christopher Fairbank and Jimmy Nail

programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey find their glamorous new jobs in a casino have a hidden downside, while Dorien rekindles an old flame

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A holiday insurance scam is foiled when investigators uncover forged documents, while CCTV footage exposes the false claims of a man who said he was hit by a bus

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, Sarah and Stewart are called to help a lady who has fallen at home, while in Glasgow, crew members rush to a woman struggling with breathing problems

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques Fair. Presenter Charlie Ross gets the lowdown on the resurgence of brown furniture, before the whole team heads to Derby for some auction action

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Fortunas of Bolton invite Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers to revamp their garden in Bolton. The final result needs to have lots to entertain the energetic boys, but that is not the only requirement - Luciano is from Sicily and would love a garden that reminds him of home, complete with a pizza oven

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Bedfordshire with a sociable couple who have £500,000 to find the right country home to suit them and their pooches and to house their very own bespoke garden pub. Aside from houses, Margherita discovers the county's agricultural pedigree when she encounters some wonderfully preserved vintage tractors

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Cousins Joe and Alex, who have both experienced sight-loss problems, compete in the culinary contest. They both love to cook, and Alex inparticular has had to adjust to cooking by using some nifty kitchen gadgets, which he demonstrates to host Rylan Clark-Neal. The duo are paired up with chefs Mike Reid and Romy Gill

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with reviving a worn-down singing trophy that celebrates the musical talent of a sadly departed mother. Wood expert Will Kirk works wonders on an ornate, wired bird cage gifted to a daughter by the biological father she only managed to track down as an adult. Plus, leather expert Suzie Fletcher wrestles a beaten-up boxing bag back into shape

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: HRH The Duke of Edinburgh Remembered
programme.desc: On the eve of His Royal Highness The Duke of Edinburgh's ceremonial funeral, Huw Edwards introduces live coverage from Windsor Castle. He hears from the people who are playing key roles in tomorrow's ceremonial procession and funeral service and is jjoined by guests who knew the Duke, and royal experts who share their thoughts on a remarkable life of duty and service

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Jackie and her husband Neil bought Jackie's childhood home in Hereford from her father Mervyn so that they could all live together and care for him in his old age. With a limited budget to spend, designers Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry RichCharlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich come up with ideas for creating a garden sculpture and focal points on a shoestring

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush takes drastic action, Martin faces a big decision and Lola confronts Isaac to ask why he took Patrick's phone. Tiffany overhears two girls talking about Keegan in the bar, having seen him online - but her confidence is shattered when they criticise his wife, prompting her to make a big blunder that leaves her in hot water

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and director Adrian Dunbar hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Katherine Ryan and Sunday Times journalist Tim Shipman joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. Guests including Richard Osman, Claudia Winkleman, Samantha Morton, AJ Odudu, Miles Jupp, Laura Whitmore, Lou Sanders, Chris McCausland, Sara Barron, Raj Bisram, Bob Mortimer, Sara Pascoe, Les Dennis, Mr Motivator, Stephen Hendry, Sindhu Vee, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Horne join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Connelly and Miles Teller join Graham to talk about the much-anticipated release of Top Gun: Maverick. Felicity Jones discusses her romantic drama The Last Letter from Your Lover, Wunmi Mosaku chats about Bafta-nominated His House, and John Bishop promotes his tour Right Here, Right Now. Plus, music from Years & Years - fronted by It's a Sin's Olly Alexander

programme.title: True Story (2015)
programme.desc: Disgraced New York Times journalist Michael Finkel discovers that Christian Longo, a man accused of murdering his family, has assumed his identity. Iintrigued, the reporter begins meeting with Longo, though as time goes on realises how dangerous his doppelganger really is. Jonah Hill, James Franco and Felicity Jones star in this thriller, based on Finkel's chilling, real-life story

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: The Duke: In His Own Words
programme.desc: A compilation of interviews with the Duke of Edinburgh in his home at Windsor, originally recorded for a documentary on the royal estate. He chats about how the castle has been transformed since he first arrived in 1952, and shares his thoughts on his predecessor Prince Albert, building a polo club on the grounds, and his successes and failures as an early eco-pioneer

programme.title: The Funeral of HRH The Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces live coverage of the ceremonial funeral of HRH The Duke of Edinburgh from Windsor. Her Majesty The Queen, together with other members of the Royal Family will attend the funeral service. A full military procession and members of the Royal Family will accompany the coffin, as it makes the final journey from Windsor Castle to St George's Chapel. Throughout the afternoon, Huw will be joined by special guests including Sir David Attenborough, Baroness Scotland, Alan Titchmarsh, and Baroness Grey-Thompson, to remember the man behind the crown. Royal experts share their thoughts and analysis on this historic occasion and pay tribute to The Duke of Edinburgh's life of service and duty. JJ Chalmers and Sophie Raworth in Windsor will report on the events from key locations around the Castle, and will meet people participating in today's ceremonial events

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City (Kick-off 5.30pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium. The Blues were defeated by Arsenal in last season's final and last lifted the trophy in 2018, while the Citizens prevailed a season later when becoming the first team to win the domestic treble, a feat they are aiming to repeat this this time around, and whichever team progresses will fancy their chances in the final against Leicester City or Southampton. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Micah Richards

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts this mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Nadine Coyle, as they assist two contestants from London in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Faith finally reaches breaking point as her family unit falls apart. Marty battles with his conscience when he realises his mother has been suffering in silence, and goes to Dylan in search of answers. Iain puts himself on the line to protect Jan, who is terrified that Ross will call her again

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: The search for information about Rose's past leads Faith to seek out an old friend from the Metropolitan Police, but she is taken aback when she discovers just how dark her mother's secrets are. The discovery prompts her to open up about a disturbing incident when she was 15. Osian's case progresses to the Court of Appeal in London - the last chance for justice - and Evan continues to keep a close eye on his ex-wife

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Newcastle United v West Ham United at St James' Park and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Sheffield United at Molineux. The Magpies and Hammers are in need of points for differing reasons, with Newcastle battling to avoid relegation and West Ham hoping to qualify for Europe. Meanwhile, Wolves will hope to avoid become embroiled in their own fight for survival and took on a team that looks almost certain to go down

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Micah Richards and Alan Shearer count down the top 10 strike partnerships in Premier League history

programme.title: Westworld (1973)
programme.desc: At a huge theme park, guests can act out their fantasies in the company of lifelike robots. However, one tourist is left fighting for survival after the mechanoids begin to malfunction, and a sinister, seemingly unstoppable gunslinger sets its sights on killing him. Sci-fi thriller, starring Yul Brynner, Richard Benjamin and James Brolin

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Newcastle United v West Ham United at St James' Park and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Sheffield United at Molineux. The Magpies and Hammers are in need of points for differing reasons, with Newcastle battling to avoid relegation and West Ham hoping to qualify for Europe. Meanwhile, Wolves will hope to avoid become embroiled in their own fight for survival and took on a team that looks almost certain to go down

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, when ordinary people do extraordinary things

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore an impressive new build in Cottishall, Norfolk, a terraced house in Bootle in Liverpool and a house with an outside toilet in Maidstone, Kent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams search for antiques in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire. Host Natasha Raskin Sharp views a special ceiling painting at Grimsthorpe Castle

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins talks to The Archbishop of York, Stephen Cottrell, about the role of Christianity after lockdown, and comedian Frank Skinner reflects on his relationship with God

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton guides a pair of budding entrepreneurs who want to escape to the Cotswolds. They have a generous £900,000 to play with, but the property must have the potential to develop a boutique country holiday let. Alistair also spends an exhilarating day at the University of Bath as he launches himself on to the UK's only bobsleigh and skeleton push-start track

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team reclaim items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with Jay Blades attempting to rejuvenate a set of classic dining chairs

programme.title: Bill (2015)
programme.desc: William Shakespeare is determined to become a successful playwright after getting kicked out of a folk band. Heading to London to seek his fortune, he gets caught up in a plot by the king of Spain to blow up Elizabeth I. Historical comedy with the cast of Horrible Histories, starring Mathew Baynton and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher goes wild in the Hambleden Valley in Buckinghamshire, on a whistle-stop tour of a chocolate-box landscape that has been the backdrop for many a TV drama and film. He also finds out how the old cottage industry of lace-making is poised for a comeback, and saddles up to meet a farming couple opening up their farm to enable safer riding in this corner of the countryside. Adam Henson is busy with some new arrivals and, as picking season dawns, Charlotte Smith investigates whether UK farmers can cover a shortfall in the number of seasonal farm workers needed to gather their harvests

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Newby Hall, near Ripon in North Yorkshire, Treasures include a letter sent by Roald Dahl to a primary school, a Chippendale chair worth a king's ransom and football memorabilia harking back to the early days of the beautiful game - when a week's wages at Arsenal were less than the price of a cup of coffee today

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Controversial plans are afoot to introduce a private clinic to Nonnatus House. The proposition leads to the first disagreement in 20 years between Sister Julienne, who sees the potential to generate income, and Dr Turner, who opposes private healthcare on principle. Shelagh feels caught in the middle of their conflict, while Trixie is delighted by the opportunity of working at the new clinic. Sister Monica Joan's crisis of faith continues to trouble her, a baby is born with no legs below the knees, and Lucille helps Cyril find the perfect accommodation that will benefit him and the Church

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: AC-12 link Gail Vella's murder with a historic case of police corruption, and Kate hatches a plan to tell once and for all if Jo is bent. They are closer than ever to cracking the case, but when Hastings' authority is undermined, it leaves his team in a potentially dangerous situation. Starring Vicky McClure, Kelly Macdonald and Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from Arsenal v Fulham at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Burnley at Old Trafford in the Premier League, as the Gunners and United both looked to complete doubles over their opponents following away wins earlier in the campaign

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Leicester City v Southampton. Action from the concluding semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love and Jamali Maddix are joined by reality star Spencer Matthews and model Vogue Williams, aiming to identify the real Mitch from a line-up of four. Mitch is a interiors enthusiast, who has lovingly created a green-heavy home in Hinckley near Leicester. Hosted by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Returning home from South Africa, the latest potential buyer has a budget of £450,000 and heads west to Cornwall, with Steve Brown leading the search for a character property that has holiday let potential. Steve also pays a visit to the Newlyn Copper Works and has a bash at transforming the striking reddish metal into a delicate work of art

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First to arrive at the barn are sisters Christine Hopkins and Pauline Samways from Kidderminster, who bring in a miniature bicycle built by their grandfather. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West takes receipt of a small mosaic crucifix with an extraordinary story, and Steve Fletcher turns his attention to an army issued box kite. Finally, antique toy enthusiast David Burville sets to work restoring a Victorian acrobat toy

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Aaron, Sarah and Yasmin must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Elizabeth and Sean need builders to repair a DIY bodge job on their fireplace in Stretford, while first-time buyer Sam wants his tired kitchen in Battersea transforming

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George agrees to go camping with Katie, but their ideas of what that entails are very different and they end up being followed on the trip by half the people they know

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: The arrival of summer in Yellowstone brings a new challenge for the beavers as their river dries up, forcing the colony to move. As food becomes scarce, the wolves have a surprising strategy to keep their pups fed, while grizzly bears pay an unexpected visit to a cowboy ranch. By midsummer, the hot and dry conditions create a new danger as deadly wildfires threaten to engulf a family of great grey owls. Presented by Kate Humble and Patrick Aryee

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall dives beneath the surface of Australia's Great Barrier Reef to examine the conditions that allowed a tiny coral block to create the largest living structure on the planet. Plus, he discovers how it continues to flourish despite the coast's storms and nutrient-poor water

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey value antiques at Southwell Minster, near Nottingham, and presenter Paul Martin finds out how the minster has had a cavalcade of historical figures pass through it, from Cardinal Wolsey to Charles I

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dr Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A vintage car - once a father-and-son project but now rusting away - and a cake stand with huge sentimental value are among the objects transformed into functional items

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the nation's lochs, discovering the secrets of these iconic features of the Scottish landscape

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Wales must go head to head and cook their six-course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice? They serve a panel of judges and guest judge Hugh Hunt from the world of maths and engineering, whose skills are used to work out how to do daredevil stunts on TV

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the cottage garden at Longmeadow, Monty Don offers advice on what to plant under shrubs and roses. On a visit to Dunsborough Park in Surrey, Carol Klein celebrates a spectacular display of tulips, and Kate Bradbury goes through the first stages of creating a wildlife garden in Brighton

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Kelly Osbourne, Ade Edmondson, Sara Pascoe, Jermaine Jenas and Nicola Coughlan play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Faisal Islam

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Cally is horrified when she discovers the truth about the four Cylons on board Galactica, and takes a drastic course of action. Meanwhile, Lee's political career gets off to an uncertain start, the divisions within the Cylon fleet lead to violence and Boomer suffers from divided loyalties

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Gaius Baltar begins to openly promote belief in the Cylon god, setting off a debate on religious freedom. Colonel Tigh, Galen Tyrol, and Tory Foster argue over how far they should go to ensure their secret is not revealed. Sci-fi adventure, starring James Callis, Michael Hogan, Aaron Douglas and Rekha Sharma

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Joe Crowley obtains data that reveals how some major water companies have been illegally dumping untreated sewage in rivers in the UK. He meets local people campaigning for a wholescale clean-up and exposes one company discharging sewage without a permit

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: The story of the first year of the pandemic told by people from all over the world. It begins on New Year's Eve 2019, when huge crowds gathered to celebrate. Contributors include Xie, a young food blogger from Wuhan whose wedding took place a few days before her city went into lockdown. As the virus travels to Europe, Dr Amie from Coventry recalls watching in disbelief the horrific scenes of people dying in Italy

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: A special Christmas edition with captains Shani and Harvey joined by guests Jarred Christmas, Elf Lyons, Ed Petrie and Sikisa Bostwick-Barnes to battle it out in festive games

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Cammy, an 11-year-old boy with sensory processing disorder, seeks a calm space in which to let his senses relax on the occasions when the bustle of family life overwhelms him

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Richie tries his hand at working on a farm with one of Britain's youngest farmers

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: To flesh out the characters' backstories, Amelia insists viewers meet all their families - with harrowing consequences

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: After Poppy eats everyone's money, they must find a way to pay the rent

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall visits Gabon, where a real-life King Kong shows him who is boss, and a scaly thief is caught red-handed

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Harsimrat and Jagjit celebrate the arrival of their first-born baby at home, while 15-year-old Ansh learns about the sacred meaning of his turban. Newlyweds Hari and Kiran continue their devotion to their guru through their marriage and in their daily lives, and Hardeep scatters his father's ashes over flowing water to unite him with God

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents the cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: The Great British Bake Off 2015 champion Nadiya Hussain embarks on a culinary road trip around Britain, meeting with producers, farmers and fishermen across the country as she creates her own recipes inspired by their produce. In the first episode Nadiya begins her tour in the Home Counties, where she visits an asparagus farm in Oxford, before trying her hand at food smoking in Milton Keynes

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Chelsea v Manchester City and Leicester City v Southampton in the semi-finals of the FA Cup

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day one at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where two more first-round matches get under way in the showpiece tournament on the snooker calendar, featuring Stephen Maguire v Jamie Jones and Neil Robertson v Liang Wenbo

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team visit Hopetoun House not far from Edinburgh. Antiques experts James Lewis and Adam Partridge are on hand to value items, while Paul discovers the architectural history of Hopetoun House and why it is such a unique treasure in the Scottish countryside

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Taking place in a specially built workshop nestled in Birmingham's historic Jewellery Quarter, Katherine Ryan hosts as eight talented jewellers battle it out to prove they can be the next big jewellery-making sensation. In each episode, the competitors are faced with two very different challenges, with their work judged by are two of the biggest names in the business, Solange Azagury-Partridge and Shaun Leane, before one contestants leaves the competition

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the wildlife of the US state, including footage of manatees, baby alligators and miniature deer living in Florida's pine forests, coral reefs and famous Everglades wetlands. The programme considers the dangers facing the animal population, including natural disasters like wildfires, flooding and hurricanes, as well as the pressure of a growing human presence in their territory

programme.title: The Funeral of HRH the Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh
programme.desc: Huw Edwards reflects on today's events at Windsor Castle where the ceremonial funeral of His Royal Highness The Duke of Edinburgh took place. Her Majesty The Queen, members of the royal family and a full military procession accompanied the coffin, as it made the final journey from Windsor Castle to St George's Chapel. With reporters JJ Chalmersand Sophie Rayworth

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Elizabeth Taylor's image is known around the world - she was one of the most famous women in Hollywood, but could just a handful of photos uncover new truths about someone we think we all know? This programme takes a journey through the actress's life in 10 photos, with their secrets revealed by those who were there and those who knew Elizabeth Taylor's story best

programme.title: Burton and Taylor
programme.desc: Having battled a series of personal struggles during rehearsals, former spouses Richard Burton and Elizabeth Taylor prepare to star in a 1983 production of Private Lives. Critical reception is initially poor following the opening performance, but members of the audience - picking up on the parallels between the Noel Coward comedy and the lead actors' own situations - respond far more enthusiastically. Drama based on real-life events, starring Dominic West and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Elizabeth Taylor: England's Other Elizabeth
programme.desc: A profile of the double Oscar-winner filmed around the time of her visit to Britain in 2000, when she received a damehood from the Queen. The actress recalls her early days as a child star in Lassie Come Home and National Velvet, and discusses working with the cream of Hollywood talent in the 1950s and '60s - including Richard Burton, whom she married twice. With contributions by Shirley MacLaine, Rod Steiger and Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the evening session on the opening day, where Ronnie O'Sullivan's first-round match against Mark Joyce reached a conclusion

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: An in-depth focus on the opening day at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, as the season-ending championship got under way with the best-of-19-frames first-round matches

programme.title: The Duke: In His Own Words
programme.desc: A compilation of interviews with the Duke of Edinburgh in his home at Windsor, originally recorded for a documentary on the royal estate. He chats about how the castle has been transformed since he first arrived in 1952, and shares his thoughts on his predecessor Prince Albert, building a polo club on the grounds, and his successes and failures as an early eco-pioneer

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets members of the community of Breaston in Derbyshire, as they aim to create a centrepiece Bug Hotel in their first entry into the Britain In Bloom competition

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the cottage garden at Longmeadow, Monty Don offers advice on what to plant under shrubs and roses. On a visit to Dunsborough Park in Surrey, Carol Klein celebrates a spectacular display of tulips, and Kate Bradbury goes through the first stages of creating a wildlife garden in Brighton

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap and Margherita Taylor look at the impact of canals on the history of the British countryside. They the Grand Union Canal and celebrating the 200th anniversary of the Regent's Canal, a once-commercial route that now brings a slice of countryside to the city, benefitting both residents and wildlife. Plus, wildlife cameraman Jack Perks documents some frogs, and an update on the fortunes of a family staking their future on the success of high-tech farming

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits Tentsmuir National Nature Reserve in Fife, to learn how the reserve has coped with the dramatic surge in visitors during lockdown, as local people reconnected with the nature on their doorstep. He also assesses how the farming industry is coping post-Brexit. Euan Mcilwraith looks back on the lessons learned as we mark the 20th anniversary of the foot-and-mouth outbreak, while in Sutherland Anne Lundon finds out what it was like to attend the remotest school in Britain. Arlene Stuart reports from the banks of the Forth, hoping to catch sight of a humpback whale called Barney

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Six budding gardeners and families will make regular appearances throughout the series to share hints from their own gardens all over Scotland to encourage us all to get growing. Mairi and Carole give Beechgrove a good old spring clean. While George is down at his allotment, Kirsty takes a day out at the beach to go searching for seaweed

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Wales must go head to head and cook their six-course menus again, but will they take on board their veteran's advice? They serve a panel of judges and guest judge Hugh Hunt from the world of maths and engineering, whose skills are used to work out how to do daredevil stunts on TV

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day two at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of Yan Bingtao's first-round contest against Martin Gould and the opening frames of Anthony McGill v Ricky Walden

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The return of the sewing competition in which 12 keen amateurs take part in three challenges, all judged by Savile Row tailor Patrick Grant and fashion designer Esme Young. The sewers start in their efforts to impress with the first pattern challenge, creating the simple, clean lines of the versatile classic sleeveless blouse called a 'shell top', and, for the transformation challenge, they must turn old T-shirts into completely new garments for men, women or children, in just 90 minutes. In the made-to-measure, mannequins are replaced with real-life models, but who will whip up a well-fitting buffet dress and win themselves garment of the week, and whose hopes of staying in the competition will flounder? Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day two at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where David Gilbert concludes his first-round contest against Chris Wakelin and four-time champion John Higgins gets his campaign under way against Tian Pengfei

programme.title: Down the Mighty River with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the two-part programme, the naturalist travels down the Baliem River's lower gorge, one of the most dangerous stretches of white water in the world surrounded by jagged rock walls. His exploration of the surrounding jungle leads to an encounter with a tribe that has had little contact with the outside world. As he concludes his trek in the river's coastal reaches, he faces a crisis of conscience as he witnesses a traditional crocodile hunt

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms around Scotland, from Argyll to the Outer Hebrides. This time, a new family of dairy farmers, the McLeans, are introduced. Meanwhile, Raymond goes the extra mile to save his lambs, Stevie faces buffalo cow number 666, and Kirsty and Aimee take last year's calves to market

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan visits Bangladesh's Ganges delta, which is under threat from increasingly extreme weather, and joins a government effort to plant huge new areas with mangroves to defend the low-lying country from tidal surges. He then travels to the kingdom of Bhutan to discover how melting Himalayan glaciers affect the entire world, but also hears from the prime minister how his country has become carbon negative

programme.title: Australia: Earth's Magical Kingdom
programme.desc: How the country's surrounding oceans - the Southern Sea, the Pacific and the Indian Ocean - create a unique environment for numerous creatures. In the waters off south Australia, sealions, once a rare sight, are now thriving, while in the wide shallows of Spencer Gulf, a mid-winter gathering of colourful giant cuttlefish battle for mates. Off the west coast, in the warm currents of the Indian Ocean, brown boobies build makeshift nests, while further south tiger sharks sweep in to prey on a whale carcass. Narrated by Barry Humphries

programme.title: I, Tonya (2017)
programme.desc: Ice skater Tonya Harding rises through the ranks at the US figure skating championships, but her future in the sport is thrown into question by a shocking incident after her ex-husband steps in. Director Craig Gillespie's comedy biopic, starring Margot Robbie, Sebastian Stan, and Allison Janney, who won a Bafta and an Oscar for her performance

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day two in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of Stephen Maguire v Jamie Jones and Neil Robertson against Liang Wenbo

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: A look back at the first-round matches on the second day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Claire becomes increasingly suspicious of Samuel's motives, and Hiro returns to Japan, where he urges Ando to help him rescue Mohinder from a psychiatric institution. Peter struggles to cope with Nathan's traumatic death, and Noah launches his plan to destroy the carnival, helped by Edgar. Drama, starring Hayden Panettiere

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Sylar seeks out the carnival to obtain a batch of powers, but discovers a new weakness. Meanwhile, Claire and Peter try to resolve their emotions at Nathan's wake, and Noah and Lauren capture Edgar in an effort to determine what he knows. Starring Zachary Quinto

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs a topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the viirtual audience

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The outcome of his compensation case against the hospital inspires Dominic to refashion his life, but when his plans are dashed, he's soon asking a huge favour of Sacha. Kian's bromance with a troubled patient takes a surprising turn when he and Chloe make a shocking discovery in theatre, while money matters continue to mount for Max

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Exotic Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of archive performances by international artists who found success in the UK charts, including Greek crooner Demis Roussos, French star Vanessa Paradis, Swedish singer Sylvia and Romanian pan-pipe player Gheorghe Zamfir

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: Performances by new wave bands from the BBC archives, including Elvis Costello, the Police, Ian Dury and the Blockheads, Squeeze, Blondie, Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers, the Cars, Patti Smith and Iggy Pop

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on November 29, with performances by artists including Dream Warriors, Dimples D, Chris Isaak, Pet Shop Boys, Soul II Soul featuring Kym Mazelle, Megabass, Patrick Macnee and Honor Blackman, Bombalurina featuring Timmy Mallet, Vanilla Ice and Deee-Lite

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the show's 1,400th edition, first aired on December 6 1990. With performances by Twenty 4 Seven featuring Captain Hollywood, the Farm, Cliff Richard, Madonna, MC Hammer, Snap, New Kids on the Block, Vanilla Ice and Betty Boo

programme.title: Duran Duran: There's Something You Should Know
programme.desc: Simon Le Bon, John Taylor, Nick Rhodes and Roger Taylor open up about the highs and lows of the band's career - with archive footage of former guitarist Andy Taylor helping round out the story. The documentary charts their trajectory through seven of their 14 albums, each one uncovering a compelling chapter in the history of five lads from Birmingham who had one simple dream - to become the biggest band in the world. Contributors include Boy George, Nile Rodgers and Mark Ronson

programme.title: Duran Duran at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of the band's performances on BBC shows

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure and Kim Appleby explore the sounds that came from Scotland, Ireland and Wales. They start in Glasgow with the American influences that shaped a substantial part of Scottish music, look at the punk and folk backdrop to Irish music and, finally, delve into the Welsh merger of folk and punk. The programme features evocative archive, music and interviews with significant figures of the era including Bob Geldof, Clare Grogan from Altered Images, Pat Kane from Hue and Cry, Moya Brennan of Clannad and Mike Peters from legendary Welsh band the Alarm

programme.title: Exotic Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of archive performances by international artists who found success in the UK charts, including Greek crooner Demis Roussos, French star Vanessa Paradis, Swedish singer Sylvia and Romanian pan-pipe player Gheorghe Zamfir

programme.title: Duran Duran: There's Something You Should Know
programme.desc: Simon Le Bon, John Taylor, Nick Rhodes and Roger Taylor open up about the highs and lows of the band's career - with archive footage of former guitarist Andy Taylor helping round out the story. The documentary charts their trajectory through seven of their 14 albums, each one uncovering a compelling chapter in the history of five lads from Birmingham who had one simple dream - to become the biggest band in the world. Contributors include Boy George, Nile Rodgers and Mark Ronson

programme.title: Duran Duran at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of the band's performances on BBC shows

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day one at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where reigning champion Ronnie O'Sullivan concludes his first-round match against Mark Joyce, and David Gilbert v Chris Wakelin gets under way. 'The Rocket' thrashed Thepchaiya Un-Nooh at this stage last year and went on to record victories over Ding Junhui, Mark Williams and Mark Selby before overcoming Kyren Wilson in the final to win his sixth world title

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: The Sicilian detective is called in to investigate the death of a man on board a Vigata fishing boat. However, as his inquiry proceeds, it becomes clear that what seemed to be an accidental fatality has far-reaching connections to more insidious criminal activity. Meanwhile, Montalbano discovers young policeman Fazio has been protecting a local family from death threats during his spare time, and as the wedding plans continue at pace, Livia and Salvo's relationship seems to have run into trouble. Crime drama, starring Michele Riondino. In Italian

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using new 3D technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Florence, and with the help of historian Ross King and the team, they discover the hidden secrets of Florence's great cathedral and its architectural genius. The pair also learn more about the Medici's fraught relationship with Michelangelo, as he fell from favour when he supported a rebellion against his own masters, and visit the world-famous Uffizi - including the Botticelli room

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on November 29, with performances by artists including Dream Warriors, Dimples D, Chris Isaak, Pet Shop Boys, Soul II Soul featuring Kym Mazelle, Megabass, Patrick Macnee and Honor Blackman, Bombalurina featuring Timmy Mallet, Vanilla Ice and Deee-Lite

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the show's 1,400th edition, first aired on December 6 1990. With performances by Twenty 4 Seven featuring Captain Hollywood, the Farm, Cliff Richard, Madonna, MC Hammer, Snap, New Kids on the Block, Vanilla Ice and Betty Boo

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: An examination of the life, music and career of legendary entertainer Frank Sinatra, told in his own words in archived interviews, along with commentary from those people closest to him. The first of two programmes focuses on Sinatra's birth to his early years as a roadhouse performer, revealing the influences behind his meteoric rise

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using new 3D technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Florence, and with the help of historian Ross King and the team, they discover the hidden secrets of Florence's great cathedral and its architectural genius. The pair also learn more about the Medici's fraught relationship with Michelangelo, as he fell from favour when he supported a rebellion against his own masters, and visit the world-famous Uffizi - including the Botticelli room

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day two at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding sessions of Stephen Maguire v Jamie Jones and Neil Robertson v Liang Wenbo. Maguire lost 10-3 to Martin Gould at this stage last year, while Robertson overcame Liang 10-5, but there was to be no repeat of his 2010 title win, as he was beaten 13-7 by Mark Selby in the quarter-finals

programme.title: Call the Midwife: The Casebook
programme.desc: Stephen McGann presents a look at the true stories behind the drama series, meeting midwives, nuns and doctors who worked delivering babies during the 1950s and 1960s to hear their accounts of the social and medical changes of the period. He talks to a former colleague of Jennifer Worth, writer of the original Call the Midwife books, and a photographer who documented the social conditions of the time. Stephen also returns to the home where he was born to share his mother's own story of childbirth

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Tony is called in to investigate complaints against an officer who was killed while on duty. Police drama, starring Neil Pearson, Tom Georgeson and Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Clark accuses Sgt Markham of corruption, while Jenny finds herself under increasing pressure over her part in the Carswell affair. Starring Neil Pearson, Tom Georgeson and Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores how the public's view of Roman Britain has changed over the decades, examining events from the initial invasion, through to the construction of Hadrian's Wall and the eventual collapse of the empire, drawing on the work of archaeologists and historians and using 50 years of BBC archive footage. With contributions from Charlotte Higgins, Adrian Goldsworthy, Neil Faulkner and Jo Quinn

programme.title: This Green and Pleasant Land: The Story of British Landscape Painting
programme.desc: Artists, critics and academics examine depictions of the British landscape in art. The programme charts the genre's development from the earliest Flemish paintings in the court of Charles I to David Hockney's digital drawings. Featuring contributions by film-maker Nic Roeg, historian Dan Snow and novelist Will Self

programme.title: Call the Midwife: The Casebook

programme.desc: Mimi is on a mission to debunk stereotypes by showing that you don’t have to be just one thing in life. She celebrates people who inspire her by following their dreams and doing what they love. One of those people is Tesha, an old school friend who runs a salon, loves hair and make-up, but also plays football in the FA Women’s National League and internationally. We also meet Nora, who has albinism and is living her dream as a fashion model. And Mimi hosts her Q&A with SBTV creator Jamal Edwards MBE, who shares his own journey to success, and how he has helped the likes of Ed Sheeran. Mimi’s family and friends make a surprise appearance for the end of the series and offer words of wisdom for anyone wanting to chase their own dreams.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Jahawi is an underwater camera operator who is passionate about preserving the coastline of Kenya, his home country. The ocean is home to a whole host of marine wildlife, but one species here is particularly under threat: dolphins. This fun and curious mammal is facing a number of issues, as their habitat is being destroyed and the fish they eat are being depleted. There is also significant human activity on this coastline, which is damaging the marine life. However, Lamu has stood the test of time and is a marine sanctuary for them, with a new port being built and things about to change. Working with the Kenyan Marine Mammal Network, Jahawi is hoping to get the area protected as a marine reserve before it’s too late. It doesn’t take long for Jahawi to find the resident bottle-nosed dolphins as they enjoy swimming at the bow of the boat, and he soon gets into the water with the dolphins to get a closer look at these magnificent creatures. here is one species of dolphin he is hoping to

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Robert wants to read a book but Justin and Little Monster really want him to play with them instead

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Raa Raa and his friends launch Huffty's whooshy whooshers into the air and lose them in the jungle - but teaming up to look for them can be just as fun as launching them

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘chicken’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We see lots of chickens – a brown chicken, a white chicken, a clucking chicken and some friendly chickens. Dee meets a funky chicken, a hatching chicken and a cock-a-doodle-do-ing chicken! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: In their adventure, Dog and Pug meet Granny Pug who makes them solve three puzzles.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The friends play on a new climbing frame, but Bing waits and watches Padno go down a slide because he is too scared to try

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The team has a very dirty floor. The cleaner starts cleaning the floor with a mop, but it is not long before a visitor with very muddy boots undoes his hard work

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin has lots of springtime fun with his friends as they visit a farm. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is very busy spring cleaning the house for Aunt Polly

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po goes for a special ride on her scooter

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Alara, Samaa, Reece, Eleanor, Ty’lana and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is are some scented lavender bags, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are smelly. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate smelly objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The children’s nursery rhyme Ring A Ring O' Roses features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring smelly things too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels find a treasure map inside Duggee's big shiny ruby, and they can see that X marks the spot

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Chloe shows Topsy a magic trick she has learnt

programme.title: Kiri & Lou
programme.desc: Lou sings a song about a flower, but Kiri's big pet rock Rockaroonie is rolling down the hill towards him! Can Kiri stop the rock in time?

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: B.O.T. tries to keep up with a family of Huggy Rollers as they roll along together. B.O.T. ends up encased in a snowball, but the snow melts away when the Rollers give it a big warm hug!

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Betty the Mouse’s little ones see a big shadow from their cosy nest in the potting shed, and all agree it must be a dinosaur. Word spreads around the garden and reaches Tilly the Tortoise, who is on a visit for the holidays. She can’t believe her luck – she loves dinosaurs! Tilly heads off on a search around the garden, bumping into Casey the Badger and Rosie the Rabbit, but no one has seen it. Auntie the Tawny Owl points her in the right direction – back to the potting shed! Will she ever find the dinosaur?

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on a special walk through the town that starts in the daytime but finishes with a torch game in the dark. On the way they spot a spiky tree, have a close-up look at a daisy, light up their reflectors in the dark, and even make sounds like the sea!

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: We learn about objects that are colourful like a rainbow, from chameleons to kites, and explore the deeper meaning of rainbows. The episode concludes with a specially written poem about rainbow colours.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Anteater has a problem: his tongue is tied up in a knot!

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a magic coat rack and dancing toilets, and watch as Book Girl makes a music organ

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 19-month-old Jac and Jemima as they discover the world around them and start to learn how to care for themselves. Jac brushes his hair as he gets ready for the day, and Jemima chooses what she wants to wear. Once ready, Jac’s family visits a local farm shop where they choose a plant and get to pick vegetables! Meanwhile, Jemima goes to a play cafe, where she enjoys a tea party with mum and dad. Back at home, Jac and dad plant their new greenery and talk about how it’ll grow big and tall, just like Jac! Jemima finishes the day with painting flowers using her hands, but she must remember to wash them afterwards! Jac has his very first haircut - what will he think? Finally, after learning how to do so many new things, they both get ready for bed. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Baby Ben visits for the day, and Woolly shows Tig how helping her mum look after the child has its own rewards

programme.title: At Home with Mr Tumble
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is at home, blowing up balloons.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Disa's family comes from the Scandinavian country, Sweden. She is baking kardemummabullar, which is a Swedish recipe for cardamom buns. Disa makes a milk mixture, sweetened with honey, and mixes it into flour to make a dough. She then grinds her special ingredient of cardamom into a powder and adds some into the dough and the rest of it into brown sugar. The dough is flattened, the brown sugar and cardamom mix is spread onto the dough and folded in. The dough is then cut into strips and twisted and wrapped into a bun ready to be baked. Will her friends like the cardamom when it's baked in her kardemummabullar?

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: George gets locked inside Mrs Hobbs' house and Waffle has a chance to prove himself to Mrs Hobbs. Can he rescue George?

programme.title: Numberblocks

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Andy has to clear all the leaves off the path in the Amazon Dome, which is a huge task, but luckily Jen has invented a new machine to help him. It’s a superpowered leaf blower! Heading to Jungle Den to find it, Andy bumps into Mr Hammond, who is unpacking from his photography expedition to the Amazon River. He managed to photograph a particularly rare fish, which has not been named yet but belongs to a group of fish called cichlids. Mr Hammond has arranged a press conference to reveal his photo but can’t work out how to get the image off the memory card. Andy offers to drop the card over to Jen after he has finished clearing the Amazon Dome. Spotting Jen’s invention, Mr Hammond inadvertently mistakes it for a little vacuum cleaner and turns it on inside the Jung

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Jessica's family is from Ukraine, a country found in Eastern Europe. This region is known for a soup called borscht, and Jessica will be making some for her friends. Jessica cooks cabbage and spring onions in oil and then adds them to grated carrot, beetroot, potatoes and a tomato and stock mixture. All of these ingredients are combined in a casserole dish. Once they are stirred together, the casserole dish can be placed in the oven to cook. Jessica wears a traditional Ukrainian outfit and serves drinks in a beautiful drinks dispenser. Borscht is famous for being brightly coloured, so will her friends be able to guess that her special ingredient is beetroot?

programme.title: Ferne & Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: We help Kush find out all about buzzy, busy bumblebees! Ferne visits a wildflower garden to find different types of bees, while Rory and his son Sandy visit the park looking for bumblebees. They then return home to build their own bumblebee house. Plus, we find out how bumblebees help to make a third of all the food we eat by a process called pollination.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join eight-month-old Myles and Iffah as they discover the world around them and begin to gain some independence. Both children start the day with some breakfast. Myles’ mum is back to work for the first time since he was born, so he’s spending the day with dad. After breakfast, Iffah and her brother Walli play together - he does a drawing while she plays with her toy duck. Meanwhile, Myles and his dad heads to a local park, where they look at the lake and see lots of different birds. Iffah’s family go to an indoor play area, where Iffah has fun playing in the ball pool. When Myles and his dad arrive at great grandma’s house, Myles reads a book with grandma and even turns the pages all on his own! After they join up with mum, they all visit a quarry to feed some fish. Iffah finishes the day with her family at a petting farm, where she gets to stroke an alpaca.
