The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. Maybe that will encourage him back?

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly organises a sleepover at the Big Hub to raise money to fix the leaky roof, but when everyone gathers that night Magnus reveals he is scared of the dark

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: A friend helps Tim make a marble run, and Topsy suggests the girls create one too

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Thelma the dragon eats too much rainbow, and only the tail feather of the mysterious Noodle Oodle bird can save her

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley visits a family farm and learns how carrots are harvested. JB Gill heads to a spring farmers' market and sees all the seasonal produce. He also discovers how alpacas provide wool - just as sheep do - and gets to take one for a walk. Storm shares a new spring poem and reveals how flour is made from wheat

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog's newspaper boat blows away and the squirrels race to rescue it

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels and Duggee investigate when they hear a strange voice coming from the castle's spooky tower

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch causes catastrophe on Whistler Mountain in Canada in a cable car hanging from a wire stretching three kilometres across the valley, higher than the Eiffel Tower

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: At the Go Jet Academy, the Go Jetters are about to watch one of nature's most spectacular events - a solar eclipse

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: There is an amazing seahorse merry-go-round on the beach, and everyone is having lots of fun going round and round - except Bevel. When he runs away from the ride, Bob finds him, only for Bevel to admit he's nervous about going in circles. Everyone joins in to help him overcome his fear and soon he's sitting on his seahorse, Blue Yah, excited about his first turn on a merry-go-round. But now the key that winds it up has broken, so Bitz needs to work out how to get it started using wind power

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster wants to wear his favourite hat on Favourite Hat Day, but it's falling apart!

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Fruity Tapper loves fruit and uses its long pointed beak like a jackhammer to shake them out of trees. To complete the mission, B.O.T. must avoid being rattled to pieces by the Fruity Tapper’s tapping and keep out of the way of falling fruit!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin has lots of Autumn fun with his friends. While Mr Tumble is struggling to play football because lots of leaves are blocking the goal

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how bees make honey in a hive and how mugs are made from clay

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: A closer look at scuba diving gear. Mark tells us all about how his Scuba diving mask allows him to come face to face with amazing creatures under the sea, like jellyfish, dolphins and even sharks!

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Nursery adventures with Shaun the Sheep's baby cousin. Timmy's friends help him find the missing piece from his jigsaw puzzle

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Pug wants to break a world record. Dog suggests they go to the North Pole so that Pug can be the first pug to reach the Pole. They have some help from a Walrus, but when they arrive they see Granny Pug has got there first. Pug breaks another world record.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Gran busts a groove when she teaches JoJo the moves to a traditional Saint Lucian dance. JoJo finds that learning a dance can take time, until she realises she can put her own twist on it.

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: When the pair's friend Tony comes to play, their pet rabbit Wiggles scratches him on the hand

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's Jess's 'bring-a-pet' birthday party and Waffle competes with Pickles the parrot to be the star. Waffle has a surprise for everyone when he takes to the stage with Simon

programme.title: Ferne & Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Woody finds out all about superhero spiders! Ferne meets a tarantula and feeds a wolf spider at the zoo, while Rory and his son Sandy go on a spider safari in the garden and at the park. Plus, we find out how spiders are amazing predators that are great for the environment.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Phinehas and Charlie as they both wait for their newborn baby brother or sister to arrive into the world. Charlie’s mum packs a bag ready to go to hospital with dad and waves goodbye to Charlie and Grandma, whilst Phinehas’ family gets ready to pop a gender reveal balloon to find out if the next arrival will be a boy or a girl. Before the big moment, Charlie wraps up a cuddly elephant toy with his Grandma as a gift for his new brother or sister. After a long wait, it’s finally time for Charlie and Phinehas to meet their new siblings. Both babies travel home to their families and meet their big brothers for the very first time. Once Phinehas meets the new baby, he joins in with dad to register the birth of their new baby. Charlie meets a health visitor, who has come to visit to check on the baby and make sure it is staying healthy and strong.

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Ideas for activities to explore our senses together. Learn how seeing, touching and smelling things are an important part of your toddler’s development. Join in with Heads, Shoulders, Knees and Toes! Hosted by Giovanna from her own home.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Barry demonstrates just how daring he really is by exploring the wonders beneath a magical trapdoor, but Kari is unsure about his tales of a land filled with glitter and unicorns

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour white, from snow to space shuttles, and explore how the colour white is used. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour white.

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie to discover the plants that we use in our everyday lives and how we can help to look after them and our planet. We use plants like bamboo to build with, we make clothes from plants, and we even turn plants into useful materials like rubber for making wellington boots. And wood we use for musical instruments! Along the way, we discover how latex comes from inside a rubber tree, and Maddie goes high into the rainforest canopy to show us how tall a bamboo plant grows. She also makes a bowl from a banana leaf.

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Arthur Sleep is joined by team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt in this slapstick quiz show featuring guests and clips from Gigglebiz. Joining Gail and Keith for today’s GiggleQuiz are Wizard Tripwick and Captain Adorable. Round 1 features clips starring Enrico Paso Doble cleaning windows and Keith Fitt trying his hand at karate, only to be bothered by a pesky fly. At the end of Round 1, Keith is annoyed to see that the fly from the clip has followed him into the studio. Always on hand to save the day, Captain Adorable adorns his Adora-Goggles and attempts to catch the fly in his net. Unfortunately, Captain Adorable mistakes Keith for the fly and chases him around the studio instead! There are more flies in Nana’s Panto Showdown as Nana and her Spiders try to get as many flies as possible stuck in their giant sticky web. At the halfway point, Gail’s team are in the lead with two gigglepoints. Round 3 features a clip starring Gail Force at a campsite. Just as Keith thinks the fly has

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: The feisty seven-year-old feels ignored when her mum and dad arrive home with baby sister Flora, so she storms out of the house and even falls out with her teddy bear

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Music Lady needs Sarah and Duck to help her return a music-loving stowaway back to the Cloud Tower

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Charlie is embarrassed when Lola refuses to take off her alligator costume and insists on wearing it everywhere she goes

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee trips and hurts his leg. Mo makes it better with a special plaster and some special mum fussing. Tee realises that he quite enjoys the special treatment and begins to milk it, but will Mo cotton on?

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny's radio hat develops a fault, which means she cannot listen to her favourite nightly lullaby, so she decides to make up her own song instead

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipine children don the Tombliboos' trousers to play a game of jumping

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Anne-Marie Duff reads Dino-Mummy, by Mark Sperring and Sam Lloyd

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts reports from a derelict semi in Dartford, Kent, while Martel falls in love with a characterful terrace in Stoke-on-Trent. Lucy Alexander is at an old water booster station in Buckinghamshire, where there are grand plans to convert it into a family home

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A holiday insurance scam is foiled when investigators uncover forged documents, while CCTV footage exposes the false claims of a man who said he was hit by a bus

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, Sarah and Stewart are called to help a lady who has fallen at home, while in Glasgow, crew members rush to a woman struggling with breathing problems

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques Fair. Presenter Charlie Ross gets the lowdown on the resurgence of brown furniture, before the whole team heads to Derby for some auction action

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Fortunas of Bolton invite Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers to revamp their garden in Bolton. The final result needs to have lots to entertain the energetic boys, but that is not the only requirement - Luciano is from Sicily and would love a garden that reminds him of home, complete with a pizza oven

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Bedfordshire with a sociable couple who have £500,000 to find the right country home to suit them and their pooches and to house their very own bespoke garden pub. Aside from houses, Margherita discovers the county's agricultural pedigree when she encounters some wonderfully preserved vintage tractors

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Cousins Joe and Alex, who have both experienced sight-loss problems, compete in the culinary contest. They both love to cook, and Alex inparticular has had to adjust to cooking by using some nifty kitchen gadgets, which he demonstrates to host Rylan Clark-Neal. The duo are paired up with chefs Mike Reid and Romy Gill

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with reviving a worn-down singing trophy that celebrates the musical talent of a sadly departed mother. Wood expert Will Kirk works wonders on an ornate, wired bird cage gifted to a daughter by the biological father she only managed to track down as an adult. Plus, leather expert Suzie Fletcher wrestles a beaten-up boxing bag back into shape

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: HRH The Duke of Edinburgh Remembered
programme.desc: On the eve of His Royal Highness The Duke of Edinburgh's ceremonial funeral, Huw Edwards introduces live coverage from Windsor Castle. He hears from the people who are playing key roles in tomorrow's ceremonial procession and funeral service and is jjoined by guests who knew the Duke, and royal experts who share their thoughts on a remarkable life of duty and service

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Walking the Pembrokeshire coast, from Newport to Fishguard, weatherman Derek Brockway gets wet - from rain, an overturned kayak in the sea and from being in a hot-tub with a view. He also learns how the last invasion of Britain was foiled by a feisty woman. Archaeologist Dr Erin Lloyd-Jones explores the link between Stonehenge and the Preseli mountains and party-planner Onkar Singh Purewal visits a Hansel and Gretel-style house, covered in shells

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush takes drastic action, Martin faces a big decision and Lola confronts Isaac to ask why he took Patrick's phone. Tiffany overhears two girls talking about Keegan in the bar, having seen him online - but her confidence is shattered when they criticise his wife, prompting her to make a big blunder that leaves her in hot water

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and director Adrian Dunbar hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Katherine Ryan and Sunday Times journalist Tim Shipman joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian turns his hand to the intricate business of cake art, soon discovering that the devil is in the fiddly detail. A prestigious competition is on the horizon, offering the opportunity for Rhod's ambitious creation to wow the judges - or make people laugh for all the wrong reasons

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Welsh Liberal Democrats

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Connelly and Miles Teller join Graham to talk about the much-anticipated release of Top Gun: Maverick. Felicity Jones discusses her romantic drama The Last Letter from Your Lover, Wunmi Mosaku chats about Bafta-nominated His House, and John Bishop promotes his tour Right Here, Right Now. Plus, music from Years & Years - fronted by It's a Sin's Olly Alexander

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is challenged to live with a capsule wardrobe - selecting just 14 items from her vast collection of clothes and wearing nothing else for two weeks. She discovers what becomes of discarded clothes with a visit to a landfill site and investigates the work of online influencers in keeping the public hooked on buying the latest new look

programme.title: True Story (2015)
programme.desc: Disgraced New York Times journalist Michael Finkel discovers that Christian Longo, a man accused of murdering his family, has assumed his identity. Iintrigued, the reporter begins meeting with Longo, though as time goes on realises how dangerous his doppelganger really is. Jonah Hill, James Franco and Felicity Jones star in this thriller, based on Finkel's chilling, real-life story

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: The Duke: In His Own Words
programme.desc: A compilation of interviews with the Duke of Edinburgh in his home at Windsor, originally recorded for a documentary on the royal estate. He chats about how the castle has been transformed since he first arrived in 1952, and shares his thoughts on his predecessor Prince Albert, building a polo club on the grounds, and his successes and failures as an early eco-pioneer

programme.title: The Funeral of HRH The Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces live coverage of the ceremonial funeral of HRH The Duke of Edinburgh from Windsor. Her Majesty The Queen, together with other members of the Royal Family will attend the funeral service. A full military procession and members of the Royal Family will accompany the coffin, as it makes the final journey from Windsor Castle to St George's Chapel. Throughout the afternoon, Huw will be joined by special guests including Sir David Attenborough, Baroness Scotland, Alan Titchmarsh, and Baroness Grey-Thompson, to remember the man behind the crown. Royal experts share their thoughts and analysis on this historic occasion and pay tribute to The Duke of Edinburgh's life of service and duty. JJ Chalmers and Sophie Raworth in Windsor will report on the events from key locations around the Castle, and will meet people participating in today's ceremonial events

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City (Kick-off 5.30pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium. The Blues were defeated by Arsenal in last season's final and last lifted the trophy in 2018, while the Citizens prevailed a season later when becoming the first team to win the domestic treble, a feat they are aiming to repeat this this time around, and whichever team progresses will fancy their chances in the final against Leicester City or Southampton. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Micah Richards

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts this mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Nadine Coyle, as they assist two contestants from London in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Faith finally reaches breaking point as her family unit falls apart. Marty battles with his conscience when he realises his mother has been suffering in silence, and goes to Dylan in search of answers. Iain puts himself on the line to protect Jan, who is terrified that Ross will call her again

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: The search for information about Rose's past leads Faith to seek out an old friend from the Metropolitan Police, but she is taken aback when she discovers just how dark her mother's secrets are. The discovery prompts her to open up about a disturbing incident when she was 15. Osian's case progresses to the Court of Appeal in London - the last chance for justice - and Evan continues to keep a close eye on his ex-wife

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Newcastle United v West Ham United at St James' Park and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Sheffield United at Molineux. The Magpies and Hammers are in need of points for differing reasons, with Newcastle battling to avoid relegation and West Ham hoping to qualify for Europe. Meanwhile, Wolves will hope to avoid become embroiled in their own fight for survival and took on a team that looks almost certain to go down

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Micah Richards and Alan Shearer count down the top 10 strike partnerships in Premier League history

programme.title: Westworld (1973)
programme.desc: At a huge theme park, guests can act out their fantasies in the company of lifelike robots. However, one tourist is left fighting for survival after the mechanoids begin to malfunction, and a sinister, seemingly unstoppable gunslinger sets its sights on killing him. Sci-fi thriller, starring Yul Brynner, Richard Benjamin and James Brolin

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Newcastle United v West Ham United at St James' Park and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Sheffield United at Molineux. The Magpies and Hammers are in need of points for differing reasons, with Newcastle battling to avoid relegation and West Ham hoping to qualify for Europe. Meanwhile, Wolves will hope to avoid become embroiled in their own fight for survival and took on a team that looks almost certain to go down

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, when ordinary people do extraordinary things

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team reclaim items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with Jay Blades attempting to rejuvenate a set of classic dining chairs

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams search for antiques in Stamford Meadows, Lincolnshire. Host Natasha Raskin Sharp views a special ceiling painting at Grimsthorpe Castle

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins talks to The Archbishop of York, Stephen Cottrell, about the role of Christianity after lockdown, and comedian Frank Skinner reflects on his relationship with God

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton guides a pair of budding entrepreneurs who want to escape to the Cotswolds. They have a generous £900,000 to play with, but the property must have the potential to develop a boutique country holiday let. Alistair also spends an exhilarating day at the University of Bath as he launches himself on to the UK's only bobsleigh and skeleton push-start track

programme.title: Bill (2015)
programme.desc: William Shakespeare is determined to become a successful playwright after getting kicked out of a folk band. Heading to London to seek his fortune, he gets caught up in a plot by the king of Spain to blow up Elizabeth I. Historical comedy with the cast of Horrible Histories, starring Mathew Baynton and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Walking the Pembrokeshire coast, from Newport to Fishguard, weatherman Derek Brockway gets wet - from rain, an overturned kayak in the sea and from being in a hot-tub with a view. He also learns how the last invasion of Britain was foiled by a feisty woman. Archaeologist Dr Erin Lloyd-Jones explores the link between Stonehenge and the Preseli mountains and party-planner Onkar Singh Purewal visits a Hansel and Gretel-style house, covered in shells

programme.title: Snowdonia 1890
programme.desc: The Braddock and Jones families continue their efforts to adapt to an 1890 lifestyle, but reality begins to take its toll. Today, the children face their first day at school, the men and teenage boys go to work at the quarry, and the women look after the animals. Narrated by Matthew Rhys

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen presents another selection of small-screen clips, focusing on the things that are made in Wales, from sports cars and corned beef pasties to one-man saunas

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher goes wild in the Hambleden Valley in Buckinghamshire, on a whistle-stop tour of a chocolate-box landscape that has been the backdrop for many a TV drama and film. He also finds out how the old cottage industry of lace-making is poised for a comeback, and saddles up to meet a farming couple opening up their farm to enable safer riding in this corner of the countryside. Adam Henson is busy with some new arrivals and, as picking season dawns, Charlotte Smith investigates whether UK farmers can cover a shortfall in the number of seasonal farm workers needed to gather their harvests

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Newby Hall, near Ripon in North Yorkshire, Treasures include a letter sent by Roald Dahl to a primary school, a Chippendale chair worth a king's ransom and football memorabilia harking back to the early days of the beautiful game - when a week's wages at Arsenal were less than the price of a cup of coffee today

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Controversial plans are afoot to introduce a private clinic to Nonnatus House. The proposition leads to the first disagreement in 20 years between Sister Julienne, who sees the potential to generate income, and Dr Turner, who opposes private healthcare on principle. Shelagh feels caught in the middle of their conflict, while Trixie is delighted by the opportunity of working at the new clinic. Sister Monica Joan's crisis of faith continues to trouble her, a baby is born with no legs below the knees, and Lucille helps Cyril find the perfect accommodation that will benefit him and the Church

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: AC-12 link Gail Vella's murder with a historic case of police corruption, and Kate hatches a plan to tell once and for all if Jo is bent. They are closer than ever to cracking the case, but when Hastings' authority is undermined, it leaves his team in a potentially dangerous situation. Starring Vicky McClure, Kelly Macdonald and Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from Arsenal v Fulham at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Burnley at Old Trafford in the Premier League, as the Gunners and United both looked to complete doubles over their opponents following away wins earlier in the campaign

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Leicester City v Southampton. Action from the concluding semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love and Jamali Maddix are joined by reality star Spencer Matthews and model Vogue Williams, aiming to identify the real Mitch from a line-up of four. Mitch is a interiors enthusiast, who has lovingly created a green-heavy home in Hinckley near Leicester. Hosted by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: The Scottish Family Party for the Scottish Parliament Election on 6th May.

programme.title: HRH The Duke of Edinburgh Remembered

He hears from the people who are playing key roles in tomorrow's ceremonial procession and funeral service and is jjoined by guests who knew the Duke, and royal experts who share their thoughts on a remarkable life of duty and service

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits Tentsmuir National Nature Reserve in Fife, to learn how the reserve has coped with the dramatic surge in visitors during lockdown, as local people reconnected with the nature on their doorstep. He also assesses how the farming industry is coping post-Brexit. Euan Mcilwraith looks back on the lessons learned as we mark the 20th anniversary of the foot-and-mouth outbreak, while in Sutherland Anne Lundon finds out what it was like to attend the remotest school in Britain. Arlene Stuart reports from the banks of the Forth, hoping to catch sight of a humpback whale called Barney

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Previously unseen material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. Guests including Richard Osman, Claudia Winkleman, Samantha Morton, AJ Odudu, Miles Jupp, Laura Whitmore, Lou Sanders, Chris McCausland, Sara Barron, Raj Bisram, Bob Mortimer, Sara Pascoe, Les Dennis, Mr Motivator, Stephen Hendry, Sindhu Vee, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Horne join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Connelly and Miles Teller join Graham to talk about the much-anticipated release of Top Gun: Maverick. Felicity Jones discusses her romantic drama The Last Letter from Your Lover, Wunmi Mosaku chats about Bafta-nominated His House, and John Bishop promotes his tour Right Here, Right Now. Plus, music from Years & Years - fronted by It's a Sin's Olly Alexander

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: True Story (2015)
programme.desc: Disgraced New York Times journalist Michael Finkel discovers that Christian Longo, a man accused of murdering his family, has assumed his identity. Iintrigued, the reporter begins meeting with Longo, though as time goes on realises how dangerous his doppelganger really is. Jonah Hill, James Franco and Felicity Jones star in this thriller, based on Finkel's chilling, real-life story

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: The Duke: In His Own Words
programme.desc: A compilation of interviews with the Duke of Edinburgh in his home at Windsor, originally recorded for a documentary on the royal estate. He chats about how the castle has been transformed since he first arrived in 1952, and shares his thoughts on his predecessor Prince Albert, building a polo club on the grounds, and his successes and failures as an early eco-pioneer

programme.title: The Funeral of HRH The Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces live coverage of the ceremonial funeral of HRH The Duke of Edinburgh from Windsor. Her Majesty The Queen, together with other members of the Royal Family will attend the funeral service. A full military procession and members of the Royal Family will accompany the coffin, as it makes the final journey from Windsor Castle to St George's Chapel. Throughout the afternoon, Huw will be joined by special guests including Sir David Attenborough, Baroness Scotland, Alan Titchmarsh, and Baroness Grey-Thompson, to remember the man behind the crown. Royal experts share their thoughts and analysis on this historic occasion and pay tribute to The Duke of Edinburgh's life of service and duty. JJ Chalmers and Sophie Raworth in Windsor will report on the events from key locations around the Castle, and will meet people participating in today's ceremonial events

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City (Kick-off 5.30pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium. The Blues were defeated by Arsenal in last season's final and last lifted the trophy in 2018, while the Citizens prevailed a season later when becoming the first team to win the domestic treble, a feat they are aiming to repeat this this time around, and whichever team progresses will fancy their chances in the final against Leicester City or Southampton. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Micah Richards

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts this mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Nadine Coyle, as they assist two contestants from London in picking out the best singers and winning up to £10,000

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Faith finally reaches breaking point as her family unit falls apart. Marty battles with his conscience when he realises his mother has been suffering in silence, and goes to Dylan in search of answers. Iain puts himself on the line to protect Jan, who is terrified that Ross will call her again

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: The search for information about Rose's past leads Faith to seek out an old friend from the Metropolitan Police, but she is taken aback when she discovers just how dark her mother's secrets are. The discovery prompts her to open up about a disturbing incident when she was 15. Osian's case progresses to the Court of Appeal in London - the last chance for justice - and Evan continues to keep a close eye on his ex-wife

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the fourth round, featuring Rangers v Celtic at Ibrox, Stranraer v Hibernian at Stair Park, Motherwell v Greenock Morton at Fir Park, St Mirren v Inverness Caledonian Thistle at St Mirren Park, St Johnstone v Clyde at McDiarmid Park and Kilmarnock v Montrose at Rugby Park

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Newcastle United v West Ham United at St James' Park and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Sheffield United at Molineux. The Magpies and Hammers are in need of points for differing reasons, with Newcastle battling to avoid relegation and West Ham hoping to qualify for Europe. Meanwhile, Wolves will hope to avoid become embroiled in their own fight for survival and took on a team that looks almost certain to go down

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Micah Richards and Alan Shearer count down the top 10 strike partnerships in Premier League history

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Newcastle United v West Ham United at St James' Park and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Sheffield United at Molineux. programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, when ordinary people do extraordinary things

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Rev Louise Purdon of Bonnyrigg Parish Church, Midlothian, and Father Frank Dougan of St Anthony's and St Mark's, Rutherglen, present this week's Reflections at the Quay

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and the team reclaim items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with Jay Blades attempting to rejuvenate a set of classic dining chairs

programme.title: Sportscene Live
programme.desc: Stranraer v Hibernian (Kick-off 12.15pm). All the action from the fourth-round tie at Stair Park, where the League Two hosts look to produce an upset against their Premiership opponents. Hibs are enjoying a strong campaign so far and will have high hopes of emulating their 2016 triumph in this competition, but they will first have to be wary of complacency against the Blues

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins talks to The Archbishop of York, Stephen Cottrell, about the role of Christianity after lockdown, and comedian Frank Skinner reflects on his relationship with God

programme.title: Bill (2015)
programme.desc: William Shakespeare is determined to become a successful playwright after getting kicked out of a folk band. Heading to London to seek his fortune, he gets caught up in a plot by the king of Spain to blow up Elizabeth I. Historical comedy with the cast of Horrible Histories, starring Mathew Baynton and Helen McCrory

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher goes wild in the Hambleden Valley in Buckinghamshire, on a whistle-stop tour of a chocolate-box landscape that has been the backdrop for many a TV drama and film. He also finds out how the old cottage industry of lace-making is poised for a comeback, and saddles up to meet a farming couple opening up their farm to enable safer riding in this corner of the countryside. Adam Henson is busy with some new arrivals and, as picking season dawns, Charlotte Smith investigates whether UK farmers can cover a shortfall in the number of seasonal farm workers needed to gather their harvests

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Newby Hall, near Ripon in North Yorkshire, Treasures include a letter sent by Roald Dahl to a primary school, a Chippendale chair worth a king's ransom and football memorabilia harking back to the early days of the beautiful game - when a week's wages at Arsenal were less than the price of a cup of coffee today

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Controversial plans are afoot to introduce a private clinic to Nonnatus House. The proposition leads to the first disagreement in 20 years between Sister Julienne, who sees the potential to generate income, and Dr Turner, who opposes private healthcare on principle. Shelagh feels caught in the middle of their conflict, while Trixie is delighted by the opportunity of working at the new clinic. Sister Monica Joan's crisis of faith continues to trouble her, a baby is born with no legs below the knees, and Lucille helps Cyril find the perfect accommodation that will benefit him and the Church

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: AC-12 link Gail Vella's murder with a historic case of police corruption, and Kate hatches a plan to tell once and for all if Jo is bent. They are closer than ever to cracking the case, but when Hastings' authority is undermined, it leaves his team in a potentially dangerous situation. Starring Vicky McClure, Kelly Macdonald and Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from Arsenal v Fulham at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Burnley at Old Trafford in the Premier League, as the Gunners and United both looked to complete doubles over their opponents following away wins earlier in the campaign

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Leicester City v Southampton. Action from the concluding semi-final, which came from Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including today's clash between Aberdeen and Livingston at Pittodrie. Plus, a round-up of Friday and Saturday's ties, as Rangers v Celtic took centre-stage

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts reports from a derelict semi in Dartford, Kent, while Martel falls in love with a characterful terrace in Stoke-on-Trent. Lucy Alexander is at an old water booster station in Buckinghamshire, where there are grand plans to convert it into a family home

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A holiday insurance scam is foiled when investigators uncover forged documents, while CCTV footage exposes the false claims of a man who said he was hit by a bus

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Aberdeen, Sarah and Stewart are called to help a lady who has fallen at home, while in Glasgow, crew members rush to a woman struggling with breathing problems

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques Fair. Presenter Charlie Ross gets the lowdown on the resurgence of brown furniture, before the whole team heads to Derby for some auction action

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Fortunas of Bolton invite Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers to revamp their garden in Bolton. The final result needs to have lots to entertain the energetic boys, but that is not the only requirement - Luciano is from Sicily and would love a garden that reminds him of home, complete with a pizza oven

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Bedfordshire with a sociable couple who have £500,000 to find the right country home to suit them and their pooches and to house their very own bespoke garden pub. Aside from houses, Margherita discovers the county's agricultural pedigree when she encounters some wonderfully preserved vintage tractors

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Cousins Joe and Alex, who have both experienced sight-loss problems, compete in the culinary contest. They both love to cook, and Alex inparticular has had to adjust to cooking by using some nifty kitchen gadgets, which he demonstrates to host Rylan Clark-Neal. The duo are paired up with chefs Mike Reid and Romy Gill

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West is tasked with reviving a worn-down singing trophy that celebrates the musical talent of a sadly departed mother. Wood expert Will Kirk works wonders on an ornate, wired bird cage gifted to a daughter by the biological father she only managed to track down as an adult. Plus, leather expert Suzie Fletcher wrestles a beaten-up boxing bag back into shape

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: HRH The Duke of Edinburgh Remembered
programme.desc: On the eve of His Royal Highness The Duke of Edinburgh's ceremonial funeral, Huw Edwards introduces live coverage from Windsor Castle. He hears from the people who are playing key roles in tomorrow's ceremonial procession and funeral service and is jjoined by guests who knew the Duke, and royal experts who share their thoughts on a remarkable life of duty and service

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Jackie and her husband Neil bought Jackie's childhood home in Hereford from her father Mervyn so that they could all live together and care for him in his old age. With a limited budget to spend, designers Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry RichCharlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich come up with ideas for creating a garden sculpture and focal points on a shoestring

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kush takes drastic action, Martin faces a big decision and Lola confronts Isaac to ask why he took Patrick's phone. Tiffany overhears two girls talking about Keegan in the bar, having seen him online - but her confidence is shattered when they criticise his wife, prompting her to make a big blunder that leaves her in hot water

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and director Adrian Dunbar hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Katherine Ryan and Sunday Times journalist Tim Shipman joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: The Funeral of HRH The Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh
programme.desc: Huw Edwards introduces live coverage of the ceremonial funeral of HRH The Duke of Edinburgh from Windsor. Her Majesty The Queen, together with other members of the Royal Family will attend the funeral service. A full military procession and members of the Royal Family will accompany the coffin, as it makes the final journey from Windsor Castle to St George's Chapel. Throughout the afternoon, Huw will be joined by special guests including Sir David Attenborough, Baroness Scotland, Alan Titchmarsh, and Baroness Grey-Thompson, to remember the man behind the crown. Royal experts share their thoughts and analysis on this historic occasion and pay tribute to The Duke of Edinburgh's life of service and duty. JJ Chalmers and Sophie Raworth in Windsor will report on the events from key locations around the Castle, and will meet people participating in today's ceremonial events

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Almost 60 per cent of Africa's population is under the age of 25, with an average age of 19, making Africa the world's youngest continent. Yet, it is still run by elderly politicians who are often leaders for life. More than 30 per cent of Africa's growing youth population is not employed, not in education, nor receiving any form of training. It is the young who lie at the heart of Africa's untapped potential, yet many believe African governments are failing to create jobs for them or provide them with the skills they need to prepare for the future. Is this a generational failure that threatens the stability of the continent?

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Paula, Steve, Carol and Tom pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Coronation Street

As Faye and Craig enjoy lunch in Speed Daal, Craig's colleague Jess congratulates him on his new CID attachment. Brian reveals his intention to report Cathy's disappearance to the police

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure

programme.title: Jaws (1975)
programme.desc: A New England seaside town's tourist season is turned into a nightmare by a giant killer shark. The local police chief's efforts to protect the public are thwarted by the greedy mayor, forcing him to join forces with a grizzled fisherman and an enthusiastic marine biologist to hunt and kill the monster predator. Steven Spielberg's thriller, starring Roy Scheider, Richard Dreyfuss and Robert Shaw

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean is invited to lunch at the stately home of an aristocratic lady who isn't all she seems. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: After Grandpa Max's super smart buddy Phil discovers that a second extraterrestrial item crashed alongside the Omnitrix Team Tennyson sets off to find the second crash site before someone else does but arrive instead to find nefarious newcomer Breaker One-Nine has already beat them to the punch.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: McFly's Danny Jones and chef Ainsley Harriott join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh prepares a surprise for Rob and Margaret Isdale, a Grantham couple in their seventies who have fostered more than 150 children over the past 45 years. Alan draws inspiration from Frances Hodgson Burnett's novel The Secret Garden, creating ancient looking follies and winding paths, and even turning a normal garden shed into a mock-Tudor feature

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan is joined by Strictly Come Dancing professional dancer Anton Du Beke, who discusses cheering up the nation this summer, and horticulturist Camilla Bassett-Smith opens up her cottage garden. Plus, there is a look at birds of prey

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: The chef is joined by singer Alexandra Burke and prepares a selection of pies, including a tarragon-crusted chicken, leek and mushroom one perfect for all the family. Pastry expert Julie Jones shows off some crowd-pleasing decorations on her rhubarb pie, while Olly Smith picks a range of beers

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern is in Sheffield as he returns with the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the Scottish Grand National from Ayr and the Greenham Stakes at Newbury, plus other key races on the cards. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Mick Fitzgerald, betting news with Matt Chapman, reports from Oli Bell and Luke Harvey, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Presenter Julia Bradbury continues her epic journey across Australia, exploring all four corners as she finds out about the continent and the people who live there. Her next step is the Blue Mountains, a rugged region in New South Wales. Along the way she has a go at paddle boarding with pooches, meets Australia's favourite koala and has a chat with fashion designer Jenny Kee. She also takes a ride on the world's steepest railway, and her hike through the mountains ends with a sighting of the bowerbird

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a fire extinguisher that explodes in a builder's face, a dog that looks like Andrew Lloyd Webber and a man who runs himself over with a tractor

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: Eight celebrity families take part in the knockout tournament, hoping to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. Presented by The Chase's Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Su Pollard, Jordan Banjo, AJ Odudu and Jonathan Ross pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as Fern Britton, Iain Stirling and Mark-Francis Vandelli answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Casino Royale (2006)
programme.desc: James Bond achieves his '00' status and is dispatched on the trail of Le Chiffre, an international criminal financing terrorist organisations around the world. Bond's investigation leads to a casino in Montenegro, where he takes part in a high-stakes poker game in a bid to bankrupt Le Chiffre and put an end to his plans. Spy thriller, directed by Martin Campbell and starring Daniel Craig in his first outing as 007, with Eva Green, Mads Mikkelsen, Judi Dench and Giancarlo Giannini

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruises: All at Sea
programme.desc: A report on how the coronavirus pandemic brought the £120billion cruise industry to its knees and 100s of ships around the world to a standstill. The documentary looks at the role of cruise ships in spreading the virus and ask if passengers' well-being was put first, as it tells the story of the six extraordinary weeks when thousands of passengers ended up in quarantine

Spy thriller, directed by Martin Campbell and starring Daniel Craig in his first outing as 007, with Eva Green, Mads Mikkelsen, Judi Dench and Giancarlo Giannini

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruises: All at Sea
programme.desc: A report on how the coronavirus pandemic brought the £120billion cruise industry to its knees and 100s of ships around the world to a standstill. The documentary looks at the role of cruise ships in spreading the virus and ask if passengers' well-being was put first, as it tells the story of the six extraordinary weeks when thousands of passengers ended up in quarantine

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A ritual killing in a village church points to the work of a satanic cult, but the detective thinks local animosity might actually hold the key to the mystery. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a wealthy businessman is killed in a mysterious explosion aboard his fishing boat and suspicion falls on his son, Wycliffe feels sure the real culprit is to be found elsewhere and unearths several locals with a motive for murder. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A man's apparent suicide in an armoured car shocks the village of Morton Fendle, but things take a more sinister turn upon the discovery he was not alone when he died. Top of Barnaby and Jones's list of suspects is the victim's missing girlfriend - whose mysterious hold over the war-blighted residents could provide the key to the mystery. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: The Suspicions of Mr Whicher
programme.desc: Another chance to see the second instalment of the crime drama starring Paddy Considine as the pioneering 19th-century inspector. Having left the Metropolitan Police under a cloud, Whicher's days as a detective are behind him. However, when he saves a respectable country lady from a violent robbery in a dangerous part of London, she hires him to investigate the savage murder of her 16-year-old niece. The investigation pits Whicher against wealthy and powerful figures, as well his former colleagues in the Met, and leads to a psychiatric institution where he must confront his own demons. Co-starring Olivia Colman, Shaun Dingwall and Mark Bazeley

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old girl is found dead in her home, Brooks and Devlin cannot be sure a crime was even committed. On further investigation, an unlikely murder weapon is uncovered and a suspect identified - a recently released serial rapist. Determined the accused is guilty, Steel pursues him with every power available - pushing the boundaries of the law to the limit. Crime drama, guest starring Kevin Doyle (Drop Dead Gorgeous), with Ben Daniels

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Three people are murdered in a shop in central London and a fourth is left for dead, and Brooks and Devlin are up against the clock to find a killer who they fear could strike again. When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root

programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective investigates a suspected kidnapping and gets his first lead when a ransom note is discovered. The missing woman then turns up dead at the wheel of a crashed car, smelling of alcohol. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective applies his professional know-how to a fatal shooting, and soon begins to suspect that the dead man's brother-in-law may know more about the case than he is letting on - a theory borne out by forensic tests on the murder weapon. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The young detective is suspended from duty following a recent case. However, he is roused from his bitterness and self-pity when the body of a woman who went missing at a funfair on Cowley Green is found near his home. He resolves to conduct a secret investigation of his own, which leads him to the city's social elite and a disturbing secret hidden in plain sight. Shaun Evans stars as the young Inspector Morse in the third series of the detective drama

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Julie Roberts, a chocolate factory-worker from Birmingham, has always known she was adopted. She longs to find her mother Marion, to see what impact her teenage pregnancy had and find out what became of her. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell also investigate the case of keen biker Marc Wolfe, who knows little about his birth mother, other than that she was Jewish and her one wish was for her son to be adopted into a Jewish family. After the death of his adoptive mother, his feelings intensified and he decided to look for his biological mum. Marc wants to know why she gave him up

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Major documentary telling the inside story of the royal family and the Queen's record-breaking reign. The first episode focuses on the conflict between love and duty that faces the royals - and which has occasionally threatened to bring down the House of Windsor. Edward VIII fatefully chose love when he abdicated to marry divorcee Wallis Simpson. Charles chose duty when he married Diana. The Queen, however, has managed to find a balance, maintaining a long marriage while becoming the longest-serving monarch

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A case from 13 years earlier returns to haunt Lewis when the evidence comes under review. Graham Lawrie was incarcerated in a secure psychiatric hospital after being found guilty of the murders of three police officers, but now the findings of the forensics lab have been called into question and an appeal has been launched. As Lawrie remains locked up ahead of his day in court, PC Mark Travis is lured to an isolated location with a hoax call and falls victim to a killer who uses the same weapon as that used in the original murders

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Peter reveals he has an appointment at the transplant centre to assess if he is a suitable candidate for a liver transplant. Cathy gets a call from Dev, telling her that it would be best if she stayed away from the kebab shop for a while until the customers forget what she's done. Michael explains to James that he went ahead with the DNA test

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective applies his professional know-how to a fatal shooting, and soon begins to suspect that the dead man's brother-in-law may know more about the case than he is letting on - a theory borne out by forensic tests on the murder weapon. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Pauline finds out about Martin's imminent departure to London and confronts him over her uncertain future. Aunt Joan tries to solve her farm's money problems by opening a B&B - and ends up having to call the doctor for her first guest, local farmer Ted. Elsewhere, Bert organises a baby shower for Louisa. David Ross (The Green Green Grass) guest stars, with Martin Clunes, Stephanie Cole and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor spends his last day in Portwenn seeing an influx of patients as he prepares to close down the surgery and move to London. As he bids farewell to old friends and sets off to leave the village, he sees Louisa's teaching assistant Tasha fall from the Harbour Day stage and stops to help. But the bigger hold-up comes when pregnant Louisa is involved in a car accident. Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: High-court judge Rachel Callaghan (guest star Juliet Stevenson) is critically injured after being shot in the underground car park of her apartment. When it becomes clear a hitman was responsible, the team tries to discover who hired him. As evidence mounts against the accused, the judge's health deteriorates, and Castle is faced with a choice between friendship and respect for a person's right to die. Bill Paterson, Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Sophie, the wife of one of Kersey's friends, goes missing after an evening class and soon her tutor is found murdered. The detectives step in to investigate but the clues lead to standing stones linked to the entrance to hell in local superstition. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.desc: Wily bus driver Stan Butler thinks a new company policy that allows women behind the wheel will rob him of his precious overtime money. Enlisting the aid of conductor Jack, he does everything he can to sabotage the plan. Comedy, based on the TV series, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant, Stephen Lewis, Doris Hare, Peter Madden and Anna Karen

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A ritual killing in a village church points to the work of a satanic cult, but the detective thinks local animosity might actually hold the key to the mystery. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When a wealthy businessman is killed in a mysterious explosion aboard his fishing boat and suspicion falls on his son, Wycliffe feels sure the real culprit is to be found elsewhere and unearths several locals with a motive for murder. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A man's apparent suicide in an armoured car shocks the village of Morton Fendle, but things take a more sinister turn upon the discovery he was not alone when he died. Top of Barnaby and Jones's list of suspects is the victim's missing girlfriend - whose mysterious hold over the war-blighted residents could provide the key to the mystery. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: The Suspicions of Mr Whicher
programme.desc: Another chance to see the second instalment of the crime drama starring Paddy Considine as the pioneering 19th-century inspector. Having left the Metropolitan Police under a cloud, Whicher's days as a detective are behind him. However, when he saves a respectable country lady from a violent robbery in a dangerous part of London, she hires him to investigate the savage murder of her 16-year-old niece. The investigation pits Whicher against wealthy and powerful figures, as well his former colleagues in the Met, and leads to a psychiatric institution where he must confront his own demons. Co-starring Olivia Colman, Shaun Dingwall and Mark Bazeley

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old girl is found dead in her home, Brooks and Devlin cannot be sure a crime was even committed. On further investigation, an unlikely murder weapon is uncovered and a suspect identified - a recently released serial rapist. Determined the accused is guilty, Steel pursues him with every power available - pushing the boundaries of the law to the limit. Crime drama, guest starring Kevin Doyle (Drop Dead Gorgeous), with Ben Daniels

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Three people are murdered in a shop in central London and a fourth is left for dead, and Brooks and Devlin are up against the clock to find a killer who they fear could strike again. When a suspect is brought to trial, Steel's hopes for a relatively straightforward case are dashed when he finds himself pitted against a brilliant defence lawyer. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Amanda Root

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When police sergeant Pete Garvey is killed, the investigation proves harrowing for Devlin as he was one of his best friends. The detective discovers the officer had recently been in contact with their childhood parish priest Jonathan Nugent, who Pete claimed sexually abused him as a child. Unable to place a straightforward murder charge against Nugent, Steel and Phillips take an unusual approach to bring him to trial. Guest starring Matthew Marsh, Ruth Gemmell and Patrick Malahide, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates the suspicious deaths of five company directors, who each received a card with the words 'The Scales of Justice' printed on it prior to their demise. However, he finds his inquiries frustrated by the remaining members of the board. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: In order to investigate a villain's idea of the perfect crime, Harriet poses as a prostitute and Jim goes up in the world. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Cowley and the team present a line of defence against groups of mercenaries and guerrillas who are waging war on the streets of Britain. David Suchet and Geoffrey Palmer guest star alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry tries to help his friend Kate evict some unwanted tenants, but comes to doubt her motives. Meanwhile, Arthur is desperate to get into the property market and tries to sell Terry's flat without telling him. Guest starring Toyah Willcox and Simon Cadell

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner visit Thailand, home to the largest carp species in the world - the Siamese carp. These fish feed differently to other varieties, meaning different tactics are required to catch them

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Todd Carty, Jo Pavey, Saira Khan and Dominic Holland pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.desc: James Bond's investigation into the death of a world-renowned scientist working on solar technology draws him into a battle of wits with a stealthy assassin, who dispatches his victims with a golden bullet and has the British secret agent next in his sights. programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Holmes is plagued by a recurring dream that may have its roots in the case of Lord Robert St Simon, whose bride disappeared on their wedding day. Guest starring Simon Williams, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geoffrey Beevers, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven Ride! (1972)
programme.desc: The one-time leader of the gunslinging band, now a marshal, turns down an old ally's call for help in dealing with a gang of Mexican bandits. However, when the desperados kill his friend and his own wife, the lawman puts together a new team and sets out to take revenge. programme.title: The Magnificent Seven Ride! (1972)

programme.title: Ben-Hur (1959)
programme.desc: A Jewish prince in first-century Jerusalem is reunited with an old Roman friend, who is now the governor of Judea. However, their differing political views over local dissent leads to a bitter quarrel, and the governor uses the pretext of an accident to sell him into slavery and imprison his mother and sister. Years later, the prince returns, determined to seek revenge and find his missing family. Oscar-winning epic, starring Charlton Heston, Stephen Boyd and Haya Harareet

programme.title: Heartbreak Ridge (1986)
programme.desc: An experienced US marine sergeant returns to a former unit to help train a squad of raw recruits and prepare them for deployment to war zones. While there, he clashes with the platoon under his command, who don't see the need for his stricter methods during a time of peace, as well as his more youthful commanding officer, and also his ex-wife, with whom he wishes to reconnect. Action adventure, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Marsha Mason, Everett McGill, Moses Gunn, Mario Van Peebles, and Bo Svenson
