programme.desc: Third to host this week are husband and wife duo Russ and Karen Colman, who hope their glamping site in Cledan Valley, mid-Wales, will win over their rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lucinda Renshaw welcomes the guests to Springfield House B&B in Instow, Devon, where things get off to a good start - until one couple realise they do not have a lock on their door

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The Cimon is first and hosts Mark and Jo want to question Karen and Russ about their breakfast feedback. Next, Nigel from The Cardinal's Hat wants clarity on some cleaning and hygiene comments made by Mark and Jo. Russ and Karen from Cledan Valley are third and are keen to put Mark and Jo straight. Finally, it's Springfield House and owner Lucinda and friend Diane want to put two of the guests at ease, but it's the payments where true feelings are expressed

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge demonstrates how he maintains his land on his trusty tractor. Couple Emma and Paul have bought a chateau in northern France. Carpenter Paul crafts a safety fence for their elevated swimming pool, Emma tackles their maze, and their first paying guests arrive. Cameras also capture Clive and his family, including 20-year-old daughter Abbie, who manages their 36-bedroom chateau in the Dordogne Valley. Getting their chateau ready for the wedding season, the family tackle the leaky roof and refresh the bedrooms, but the furniture proves tricky to fit.And three sisters raised in the UK travel to France in search of the perfect chateau, but they have a very long checklist

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick helps Paul cut some Vitrolite vintage tiles for an art deco bathroom - but it proves harder than they expected. Some lime plastering back at Paul's chateau requires a search for some horse hair from a local stables. Abbie is busy painting furniture for bedrooms, and mum Karen has problems with their swimming pool, the fire extinguishers certifications all need renewing, and with a big wedding party arriving soon, the to-do list just keeps getting longer

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Couple Mariam and Johnno arrive at their new chateau with their teenage son Monty and discover that they have no running water and that builders are busy in every room. To get respite from the heat and building work, Mariam visits Angel to get help turning her designs into tiles for her new home. Abbie and her mum Karen have wallpaper decisions to make, and wrangle oversized cots as they continue to get their chateau ready for the biggest wedding party of the season

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Elvis fan Sandra and her husband Desmond seek help in their search for their dream holiday home near Mar Menor's stunning beaches on Spain's Costa Calida. They would ideally like a property with three bedrooms for when their family come to visit. Danni Menzies has five options for their £150,000 budget, but whether the couple will be impressed enough to put in an offer remains to be seen

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Devon couple Alan and Jayne Poole have been holidaying in Orlando for many years and are in love with American culture so much so that they are now ready to splash out on a holiday home in the Sunshine State. With the help of property expert Laura Hamilton and a budget of £115,000, they are hoping to find somewhere that they and their family can enjoy for years to come

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: The return of the programme property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad, beginning with Cath and her daughter Kate looking for a tranquil holiday home along the Costa Almeria in southern Spain. Cath wants enough space for her family to be able to visit, but she also wants somewhere to escape the rat race and find inspiration for her writing. Danni Menzies has five great properties lined up for her £170,000 budget, but will any be the oasis of calm that she has her heart set on?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Software engineer Andy Forsyth and his partner Jacqui Monroe from Yorkshire have three children between them. With the youngest about to fly the nest, they've decided the time is right to follow their dream and buy a second home in Barbados. Danni Menzies shows them five fantastic properties for their £400,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Pub landlady Alison seeks a home on the Costa Dorada in Spain. Famous for its golden beaches, the region has stunning river valleys and traditional hilltop villages, where Alison would love to buy a smallholding and keep chickens. With five options up his sleeve, Jonnie Irwin helps Alison and her friend Theresa, who's come along for support, find the perfect property for her £70,000 budget

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge attempt to renovate the orangery with the help of a roofing expert and some pre-loved slate, all the way from Wales. They also discover some unexpected history beneath their feet, and Angel finds two old daybeds in need of upcycling

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: An investigating officer for Bedfordshire Police deals with the case of a man left at the brink of death following a violent incident at home, and realises the victim is a man who came to see her at the station the day before - leaving her wondering if she could have prevented the attack before it happened

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson returns with the daring dating show - and this time it promises to be even more revealing as two singletons choose a date from six naked people whose bare bodies are revealed from the feet up. First up is 32-year-old wrester Iain from Manchester, who loses his nerve when he's not in the ring. Can the show find hi the perfect partner to pin him down for good? He is followed by 32-year-old graphic designer Leon from south London. Having recently lost weight, this bi-curious graphic designer is looking for the perfect partner - female or his first male - to share his new body

programme.title: Snackmasters
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix sets two chefs to the challenge of replicating the Wagon Wheel, a precise combination of mallow, crumbly biscuit and chocolate. Tristan Welch, who cut his teeth with Gary Rhodes, aims to solve the problem with madcap gadgets, while Cyrus Todiwala, one of the most respected Indian chefs in the country, takes a more instinctive approach. They have seven days to perfect their replicas before a cook-off before a panel of Wagon Wheel bosses, who choose the winner. And while they're busy in the kitchen, comedian Jayde Adams goes behind the scenes to reveal how the circular snack is really made

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan and Joe Lycett take on Johnny Vegas and Sean Lock in the words-and-numbers game, while would-be novelist Christopher Bliss joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Three-legged Labrador Dave seeks a new best friend, and his owners Laura and Paul hope that Luna, the beautiful but nervous saluki, will win Dave over. Nine-year-old stray westie called Norman gets much attention from mother and son Lorraine and Oliver - upsetting youngster Cynthia, a Staffie cross. Meanwhile, enthusiastic setter-poodle pup Obi might just win the chance to bring the joy and chaos that Mike and his partner Tracey don't yet know they need in their lives

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes a simple sausage and transforms it into a stunning baked casserole, with potato dumplings that are crispy on the top and soft on the bottom. He also prepares a warm aubergine dish dressed with homemade pesto and served on a bed of fresh salad, with a crisp, garlic flatbread on the side

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Helen Lawal heads to one of the world's biggest coffee factories to find out how instant cappuccinos get their froth. Matt Tebbutt is in Naples to discover why most tinned tomatoes come from Italy, while Kate Quilton embarks on a panettone odyssey across Europe to learn how the fluffy cake lasts so long

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week kicks off at Creates Monmouth in Monmouthshire, where a peaceful night's sleep is ruined by an early morning fire alarm and a lack of privacy bothers one guest

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals stay over at Coastal Park Accommodation in Llanelli, where an evening drinks session turns tense when Billy confronts hosts Claire and Tony about their business model

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third week's location is the Queens at Horton in Shropshire, run by Tracy and Billy Scott. The rivals are wowed by the luxury rooms - until Tracy finds a screw loose, that is

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners converge at Caroline Weaver's Whychurch Farm Glamping in Wiltshire. Kenny and Ben are pleasantly surprised by the cleanliness, but Billy and Tracy feel a little cramped in their pod. A day spent helping out on the on-site farm isn't to everyone's taste, but an evening barbecue is a massive hit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day finds the B&Bers ready to settle some scores. First to get some answers on their feedback are Kenny and Ben, who want to take a peek at Caroline's privacy issues and reveal why they found her sleep score alarming. Next, Tony and Claire confront Kenny and Ben on a 'wee problem', and chew over Billy and Tracy's breakfast score - who in turn want to clear up Claire and Tony's cleanliness comments

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul desperately need to fill their lake with fresh water ready for the artists' retreat they are hosting. Emma steps in to model Victorian outfits for the artists, while Paul struggles on kitchen duty. Angel gives Abbie and Karen tips on how to save money for their wedding business, and she shows them how to create a stunning bar

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul plan to host locals - including the mayor - to view the work they have done on the front of their chateau. However, Paul has first to design, build and, with a bit of advice from Dick Strawbridge, fit two heavy balconies from scratch. Elsewhere, Mariam and Johnno get their antique fireplace fitted, create a stylish sink out of a copper bucket, and host a 'thank you' meal for their builders

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his two-part challenge at Mangia Mangia in Woodland Park, Colorado. In order to save the struggling Italian restaurant from disaster, he must persuade harassed owner Julie to co-operate with the head chef, and work his magic on a menu that relies heavily on a microwave

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in Redondo Beach, California, for the first of two visits to Zayna Flaming Grill, a Mediterranean restaurant owned and run by aunt and niece Faye and Brenda. Faye believes she has the weight of the entire restaurant on her shoulders, while Brenda feels she isn't treated as an equal in the business. With tensions rising, the food not up to scratch and everyone playing the blame game, the chef is faced with one of the trickiest situations he's ever encountered

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leyla and Will Moore from Leeds both have hectic working lives, but now the five kids they have between them are grown up, they're ready to slow down a little and buy their very own place in the sun. With their budget of £350,000 Scarlette Douglas shows them five stunning villas on the Algarve in Portugal

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Colin and Richard from Glasgow love Playa del Ingles in Gran Canaria and have been holidaying there for over 20 years. With retirement approaching, they feel now's the perfect time to buy their dream holiday home there to escape the Scottish winters and spend up to six months of the year in the sunshine. Jean Johansson lines up five potential properties for their budget of £200,000, but will any of them seem the perfect place?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Andrew and Denise Holmes from Lancashire have been holidaying as a family in Florida for nearly 25 years. With the help of expert Laura Hamilton and a budget of £140,000 they set out to find their dream property in the Sunshine State

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Rita and Stephen from Watford have visited the lively Portuguese town of Albufeira over 40 times now and are finally looking to buy their own holiday home there. They want somewhere that's close to both the beach and all the action for their budget of £150,000. Jasmine Harman lines up five potential properties for them to choose between

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a father and son planning to convert an oil rig escape boat into a floating home, and an inventor working on a perfectly spherical house. The Spanish tour concludes with a visit to a studio apartment by the sea with rotating internal walls, and the team's expandable camper goes on its maiden voyage at the grand opening on Hastings Pier

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Steph Wilson and her husband Alex build a contemporary, cantilevered farmhouse on farmlands once owned by her grandfather in Herefordshire. The couple are told their ambitious design will cost £500,000, twice as much as they can borrow, but the pair decide to take a leap of faith and move into a draughty old caravan on site as they begin working on their new home

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Twenty-seven-year-old salesman James is paired with 31-year-old Serena, who is looking for a reformed bad boy after a history of dating disasters. Fifty-year-old cancer survivor Sara is impressed with her match, fellow fitness fanatic Peter, and 39-year-old singleton Rose opens up about her Crohn's disease to her date Jordan - something she always does on a first date to see if men are put off

programme.title: Guy Martin's Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. The presenter experiences the training of a Second World War fighter pilot, ultimately taking to the skies in a Hawker Hurricane for a dogfight against a Messerschmitt 109. In the first part, he learns how to fly in a Tiger Moth bi-plane, before progressing to the much faster Harvard aircraft and combat manoeuvres. He also tries his hand at clay pigeon shooting, which was used as a training exercise to help rookie pilots shoot fast-moving targets

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson returns with the daring dating show - and this time it promises to be even more revealing as two singletons choose a date from six naked people whose bare bodies are revealed from the feet up. First up is 32-year-old wrester Iain from Manchester, who loses his nerve when he's not in the ring. Can the show find hi the perfect partner to pin him down for good? He is followed by 32-year-old graphic designer Leon from south London. Having recently lost weight, this bi-curious graphic designer is looking for the perfect partner - female or his first male - to share his new body

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats a seven-month-old fox red Labrador puppy with lameness, caused by a joint defect called osteochondritis dissecans. He explains that medicine can be given, but in the end the dog may end up requiring a complete ankle replacement or a joint fusion on both sides. Jo has travelled 400 miles to bring her beloved four-year-old springer spaniel to see Noel. The pooch's kneecap keeps slipping in and out of her knee joint, and despite several previous procedures, the problems persist. After an examination by Noel, Jo faces the further worry of osteoarthritis

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: An investigating officer for Bedfordshire Police deals with the case of a man left at the brink of death following a violent incident at home, and realises the victim is a man who came to see her at the station the day before - leaving her wondering if she could have prevented the attack before it happened

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the most compelling and memorable stories from the past five years, beginning with a series of extraordinary births caught on camera at Chester Zoo. Rhinoceros hornbill Liv and partner Manu are yet to produce any offspring, but when they are moved to a new nest, Liv quickly produces a fresh clutch of egg. Meanwhile, clumsy seahorse Seabiscuit only has a week to attract a female, so he has time to incubate the eggs and give birth in time for the next full moon

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Helen Lawal heads to one of the world's biggest coffee factories to find out how instant cappuccinos get their froth. Matt Tebbutt is in Naples to discover why most tinned tomatoes come from Italy, while Kate Quilton embarks on a panettone odyssey across Europe to learn how the fluffy cake lasts so long

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: A wealthy man with an expensive taste for designer clothes moves in with a Swansea single father on benefits, and has to get to grips with his dislike of animals

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Braintree's waste enforcement officers crack down on dog owners who are leaving the region in a mess. Plus, Malvern waste warriors Dan and Jude hunt down bags of rotten refuse that have been washed into the local waterways

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Watford builder David Simpson has a fight on his hands when his long-term tenant moves her boyfriend in and suddenly decides to stop paying - and when he tries to evict her, she makes life difficult. In Woolwich, lawyer Chris Sharpe helps a client whose property has been taken over by squatters, who have changed the locks and claim they are legitimately renting the house

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The owner of a tanning salon regrets letting an employee move into a house he owns when he is faced with complaints from the neighbours about wild parties and £3,000 in unpaid rent. A couple managing a property for their daughter arrange to sell it, only to find the tenant refuses to leave, while a housing enforcement officer goes in search of a landlord who has left a tenant without light, heat or running water

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul, Steve and Ben make a shocking discovery when they break into a home in Essex while serving an eviction notice on a single mother and her two children. Meanwhile, Stewart and Iain receive more than just threats from the staff and owner of a taxi business, a car dealer has to choose between paying up or losing his showroom stars, and an Indian restaurant owner is in danger of losing his livelihood

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers pursue international car thieves as they try to leave the country in a stolen BMW, and police chase a drink-driver through Epping Forest. In Southend, burglary suspects fleeing back to London find themselves surrounded by multiple interceptor units

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The unit's hi-tech helicopter is called on to help pursue a motorcyclist riding at speeds of up to 130mph and showing no sign of slowing down for oncoming traffic or police cars. A petrol thief risks danger when trying to destroy evidence, and an officer tests the latest weapon in the fight against crime - a Subaru Impreza STi

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The police dog unit is called into action to chase a thief seen trying to break into a lorry, and the pursuit of a Range Rover leads the interceptors to a haul of stolen prestige cars. A seized Lamborghini brings out the team's competitive streak, and the presence of an intruder puts the station on full alert

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul Bohill and Steve and Ben Pinner face their biggest eviction yet - taking on squatters in a large central London office block. With the help of the police, they try to remove a suspected group of 70 from the building, but with a barricade in place, the team faces a challenging operation ahead. Elsewhere, officers are confronted by an army of family and friends when they attempt to enforce a writ at the property of a debtor, and face a difficult eviction at the north London home of a heavily pregnant tenant and her family, after the council fails to pay the rent to the landlord

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve make an extraordinary discovery inside a locked bedroom, and immediately call for police back-up. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town football club to recover a debt, while Paul and Ben have difficulty evicting a couple from a house in Kent after learning they have six children and nowhere else to go

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Brent surprises Aaron and David when he impresses Leila as she comes over for a home assessment. Mackenzie develops feelings for Richie

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Alf declares that he is taking Martha home, to everyone's scepticism. Susie opens a separate account for the surplus money at the surfing competition

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Richard and Brennan clean up a semi-detached house in Bedford belonging to a woman with rheumatoid arthritis, which has left her with extremely restricted mobility

programme.title: Extreme Nightmare Tenants
programme.desc: The work of lawyer Chris Sharpe, who specialises in helping landlords who are plagued by troublesome tenants and trespassers. Chris tries to remove trespassers from a landowner's one-and-a-half-acre site, filling it with up to 20 caravans and a mountain of rubbish - only for things to take a menacing turn. Business partners in Bristol dream of turning a derelict swimming baths into flats, but their dreams turns sour when squatters move in, and Chris receives a shock when he tries to evict squatters from an abandoned print factory in Essex

programme.title: Braveheart (1995)
programme.desc: Account of the life of medieval Scottish hero William Wallace. After the slaughter of his father and brother, young Wallace is taken in by his uncle, who teaches him how to use not only his brawn but his brain - which he employs to great effect years later when he resolves to drive the English out of Scotland. Oscar-winning historical epic, directed by and starring Mel Gibson. With Patrick McGoohan, Sophie Marceau and Catherine McCormack

programme.desc: Documentary using eyewitness testimony and footage from camcorders, CCTV and phones to examine traumatic, shocking, humorous and occasionally bizarre feuds between neighbours. programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: A wealthy 21-year-old with a busy social life moves in with a single mother from Orpington and her three children. She learns an important lesson in the need to become independent from her parents - and their money

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Catching up with tenants and landlords from previous episodes, including a man living in a damp and mouldy flat, who was unable to sleep in the bedroom due to the horrendous conditions. The programme also revisits a 70-year-old landlady whose tenants stopped paying the rent for a year, and the council's Rogue Landlord Team in Lewisham, who learned where a slum property owner's reputation for a short fuse came from

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A landlord seeks urgent advice from eviction specialist Chris after finding his three-bedroom terrace has been stripped bare of the fittings, the flooring, and even the grass on the lawn. Landlady Rose gets a shock when she finally gains access to her property, and Dave from Peterborough Council's housing team comes across some of the worst damp and mould he has ever seen. Plus, Selina and Lee must return from America for a court case to recover £10,000 rent arrears on their UK property

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Claire and Simon seek eviction specialist Chris's help to evict a man claiming to be renting their old family home. Meanwhile, Hamza hopes to get his council flat back after he tried to help a neighbour's friend by letting them stay for a week, but ended up being shut out when they refused to leave. Plus, a man is almost £30,000 in debt and in danger of repossession after his tenants stopped paying the rent

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Alex is hit with a £7,000 bill after his tenant is found to have been running a business from his property. Developer Jon calls in an eviction specialist to rid a disused tram station of squatters, and Vicki is surprised to find out that a tenant has stopped paying rent and moved in a sub-tenant

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The owner of a tanning salon regrets letting an employee move into a house he owns when he is faced with complaints from the neighbours about wild parties and £3,000 in unpaid rent. A couple managing a property for their daughter arrange to sell it, only to find the tenant refuses to leave, while a housing enforcement officer goes in search of a landlord who has left a tenant without light, heat or running water

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Matt and Garry try to recover more than £6,000 owed in nursery fees in Warwickshire, while Gary and Cona head to West Sussex, chasing £11,000 owed by a businessman who has defaulted on a previous plan, and have no option but to break into the property. The pair also head down to the south coast of England, seeking more than £3,500 owed for an unpaid loan

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gary and Paul visit Kent to recover £2,500 owed by a publican to a utilities supplier - only to find themselves in the middle of a family at war. Stewart and Elmor deal with a sensitive case in Liverpool, as they repossess a property where the tenant has failed to pay any rent for almost a year

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve discover a locked wooden case when they are called to evict tenants from a flat in London's Docklands. Opening it up, they realise they urgently need police back-up - and the property quickly becomes a crime scene. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town FC to collect a debt, while Paul's team arrives at a property in Essex to find that they have to remove a woman and her six children

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul Bohill and Steve and Ben Pinner face their biggest eviction yet - taking on squatters in a large central London office block. With the help of the police, they try to remove a suspected group of 70 from the building, but with a barricade in place, the team faces a challenging operation ahead. Elsewhere, officers are confronted by an army of family and friends when they attempt to enforce a writ at the property of a debtor, and face a difficult eviction at the north London home of a heavily pregnant tenant and her family, after the council fails to pay the rent to the landlord

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Another selection of neighbourly conflicts, featuring a dispute between two retired men about a new driveway, which resulted in the pair fighting in the street in the Tayside town of Kinross. The Webbs' peace in rural Plymouth was shattered when a new family moved in a few doors away, and a row about parking erupted into a full-blown punch-up. Meanwhile, Rita Grootendorst claims to have been the victim of a land grab by her next-door neighbours, although she is the one who has been arrested for assault and criminal damage over a drainpipe

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Wiltshire couple Edward Lea and his wife found their happy retirement was short-lived when an imposing 8ft breeze-block wall - dubbed the Great Wall of China - was built by their neighbour between their properties. In Scotland, Karen Ryrie and her elderly mother are fed up with living opposite John Tyer, his three dogs and a huge family of rabbits. Following their complaint about the smell, Karen's subsequent accusation of assault has made the dispute far more serious. Elsewhere, Linda Bird had not been expecting to wear headphones around the house when she moved to Bedfordshire for a quiet life, but the issue has escalated beyond excessive noise to become a battle over boundaries with neighbour Stanislav Fajdel

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A dispute between households arising from investments made into a business venture that later failed, and the fury surrounding the construction of an extension that saw damage to an adjacent property. Plus, updates on people and stories featured in previous programmes, including the latest whereabouts of rowdy rapper Elvis Turasinze, who drove his neighbours berserk with his beat-making

programme.title: Rose: Making a Monster
programme.desc: Documentary exploring Rose West's childhood, looking back at experiences in her younger years that lead to her becoming one of the most evil serial killers in British history. Throughout the programme, people who knew Rose personally - from childhood neighbours to the solicitor who represented her at the final trial - share stories from her troubled youth, and discuss how this may have impacted her in later life

programme.title: Fritzl: Surviving Evil
programme.desc: The case of the Austrian man Josef Fritzl, who kept his daughter prisoner for 24 years in the cellar of his house, until she was released in 2008 at the age of 42. Dramatically pleading guilty to all of the charges at the last moment of the trial, he was sentenced to life in a secure unit for incest, imprisonment, raping the victim - who bore seven children - and causing the death of one child by neglect

programme.title: Murder by My Fiance
programme.desc: A series of true crime documentaries, starting with the story of British millionaire Andy Bush, who was murdered when he went on a holiday to Spain. The documentary examines new evidence surrounding the case, featuring CCTV footage, police records and insightful testimonies, and also looks at the involvement of his obsessive ex-girlfriend, who was implicated in his murder

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Paramedic Jack attends the scene of a double stabbing in Birmingham, where two neighbours have been attacked. Plus, Ambulance Control receive an emergency call reporting a man trapped under a bus

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Richard and Brennan clean up a semi-detached house in Bedford belonging to a woman with rheumatoid arthritis, which has left her with extremely restricted mobility

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of a US Navy captain brings to light a link to Ducky's late mother, prompting the medical examiner to question a decision he made 37 years earlier. The team members exchange holiday gifts as Gibbs spends Christmas dinner with Fornell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team searches for new leads after an undercover operation to apprehend a businessman who apparently uses acts of terror to manipulate the stock market is compromised. Bishop and Qasim join forces to find a break in the case

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The seemingly straightforward murder of a stockbroker plunges Briscoe and Green into the murky world of insider-trading and blackmail, as they learn that the victim led a seedy double life. However, their investigations take on a new dimension when the suspected killer is himself bumped off by a professional hitman. Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The murder of a white teenager in Harlem seems like an open-and-shut case - until two patrol officers are implicated in the crime. As tensions between residents and investigating officers mount, the Mayor puts pressure on Schiff to make an example of the cops, hoping it will be enough to satisfy ADA McCoy. Drama, starring Steven Hill

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A college student is found dead, prompting the detective duo to search for a rapist on campus. However, a report from the medical examiner puts them on a different path, and they discover events may have gone down an even more sinister track

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the case of a woman found bludgeoned to death with a paving slab at a subway station. A petty thief leads them to a man with a history of mental problems, recently released from prison, who confesses to the crime and a previous murder. However, the victim's ex-husband wants to know why the suspect was walking the streets in the first place

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a violinist with the New York Symphony Orchestra and find the trail leads back to her Svengali-like conductor. However, they have a difficult job getting the case to trial, with a reluctant witness, a suspicious judge and a talented lawyer standing between them and a guilty verdict

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman's body is found in a lift, and McCoy finds his prosecution of the prime suspect hampered by a vindictive judge Ross offended in court

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: After Gibbs spots the brother of an NCIS Most Wanted suspect, he immediately assumes his former alias and goes undercover with their infamous anti-government militia. Meanwhile, Bishop and Reeves partner on an off-the-books investigation

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Palmer faces a dilemma during a hit-and-run investigation when he spots a stranger standing on a ledge, and places his own life in peril in an attempt to get the man down to safety

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a petty officer in a small town outside Washington, D.C, prompts the team to once again partner with The Sherlocks, a privately funded investigative team that includes their newest member, Anthony DiNozzo Sr. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An old family friend from the army beseeches Gibbs to join him on a road trip to search for a missing military ID bracelet. A Navy lieutenant in NCIS protective custody is poisoned by an infamous drug dealer. Dabney Coleman guests stars, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: When a woman is found dead in her apartment, her son, recently paroled after serving 15 years for murder, becomes the prime suspect. Goren and Eames meet the detective who investigated the earlier case and discover the man may have been wrongly convicted - revealing a complex cover-up that implicates the policeman himself. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate when a man's release from prison coincides with the murder of his wife and lawyer. His claims to have been the killer's real target are given a credibility boost when Goren learns that a notorious criminal might have received a hit-list that featured his son. Crime drama, with Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Jo and Lindsay investigate the murder of a successful Brooklyn-born businessman, and discover he insisted on maintaining connections with the housing project where he was raised. His wife's statement contains inconsistencies, raising suspicions about her private life - but as events unfold, it becomes clear that neighbourhood drug dealers are involved. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Sela Ward and Anna Belknap

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac is seriously wounded in a shooting. While the team hunts down the gunman in a bid to bring him to justice, the veteran detective has a vision of his late wife Claire (Jaime Ray Newman) and agonises over whether he wants to fight for life or be reunited with her in death. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Sela Ward

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Hank and Nick encounter an urban legend in the Portland sewers. Fantasy crime drama, starring David Giuntoli, Russell Hornsby and Sasha Roiz

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick, Hank and Wu search for two missing teenagers, and realise they have been taken by an evil spirit of Christmas that plans to eat them as punishment for being naughty. Elsewhere, Renards' tour of Europe brings him closer to tracking down Adalind

programme.title: Columbo: An Exercise in Fatality (1974)
programme.desc: The owner of a chain of health clubs kills a business partner who was about to expose his crooked dealings. Making the death look like an unfortunate weightlifting accident, he cunningly creates the perfect alibi using a tape recording of his victim's voice - but he reckons without the intervention of the shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Conrad and Philip Bruns

programme.title: Columbo: Rest in Peace, Mrs Columbo (1990)
programme.desc: The wife of a criminal who died in prison swears revenge on the men she holds responsible for his incarceration - including Columbo. She kills one of her targets, and the shabby sleuth is assigned to the case. As she faces his well-honed approach to sniffing out a suspect, she plots to murder his wife. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Helen Shaver and Ian McShane

programme.title: Columbo: Agenda for Murder (1990)
programme.desc: A crooked lawyer hopes to start a promising political career, but a former client he saved from prison threatens to blow the whistle on him. The attorney murders the blackmailer and makes it look like suicide - but the shabby sleuth sees through his ruse and sets out to expose the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Louis Zorich and Patrick McGoohan, who also directed

programme.title: Columbo: Double Exposure (1973)
programme.desc: A maker of motivational films based on subliminal messages uses his expertise to plant a subconscious command in the mind of a rival to lure him to his death. However, any hopes of his getting away with it are dashed by the arrival of a certain shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Culp, Robert Middleton, Louise Latham and Arlene Martel

programme.title: Columbo: Swan Song (1974)
programme.desc: A country singer arranges for his wife and his underage lover to both die in a phoney plane crash after his spouse diverts all his money to her religious causes in revenge for his affair. He is confident he remains above suspicion, but Columbo realises something does not add up and sets out to wrest a confession from the musician. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Johnny Cash and Ida Lupino

programme.title: Columbo: Try and Catch Me (1977)
programme.desc: A grieving crime writer blames her niece's husband for her death and concocts a plan to take revenge, luring him to her walk-in vault, before locking him in and leaving him to die. She then puts her knowledge of the criminal mind to good use by covering her tracks, and although Columbo strongly suspects foul play, he finds clues are thin on the ground. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and Ruth Gordon

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Dangerous Match (1973)
programme.desc: An American chess champion fears he is about to be beaten by a superior player from Russia, and decides the only sure-fire way he can avoid defeat is by murdering his opponent. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Laurence Harvey and Jack Kruschen

programme.title: Columbo: A Case of Immunity (1975)
programme.desc: A senior adviser to the king of a Middle Eastern nation plots to increase his own power by having the head of security at their American embassy killed and placing the blame on a group of dissidents. Columbo is assigned to crack the case, but the suspect's diplomatic immunity hinders the progress of the investigation. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Hector Elizondo and Sal Mineo

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation

programme.desc: Former drug-smuggler Eddie Padilla shares the story of how he managed to escape from one of Peru's most notorious prisons despite being told he would be killed by the guards if he attempted it. programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: The story of Vietnam Prisoners of War Ernie Brace and John McCain and the friendship they formed by tapping code through the cell walls of Hanoi Hilton prison in the late 1960s. The pair did not meet face to face until an event at the White House in 1973. Brace was the longest-held American civilian prisoner, detained for nearly eight years. McCain would go on to become a US senator and contest the Republican nomination for president against George W Bush

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of the double-murderer Stephen Farrow, who killed a 77-year-old teacher and a 59-year-old reverend in two frenzied attacks

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook
programme.desc: The presenter explores the case of John Straffen, who killed two young girls in the summer of 1951. He was found to be unfit to plead and committed to Broadmoor Hospital, but during a brief escape from the facility in 1952 he killed again. It was determined that the cause of his behaviour was severe brain damage, traced back to an attack of encephalitis as a child in India. When he died in 2007, he was the longest-serving prisoner in Britain

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of the German serial killer Joachim Georg Kroll, who hunted and ate parts of his victims, earning him nicknames such as 'the Duisburg Man-Eater' and 'the Ruhr Cannibal'

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: Shocking crimes in small towns and villages across Britain, focusing on the murder of taxi driver Stuart Ludlam, who was shot at point-blank range in Derbyshire in 2009

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: First of a two-part story directed by Quentin Tarantino. Stokes is kidnapped and buried alive in a glass coffin and left with only a gun and a chilling message from his abductor. His fellow officers follow a trail of clues to find him, while Willows makes a deal with her father. Tony Curtis guest stars as himself

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: Chicago Outfit enforcer Frank Cullotta discusses crime boss Tony Spilotro, including his life in the mob and the burglary that led to his capture

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: Former American contractor Roy Hallums was working in Baghdad providing food to the US Military in 2004 when a masked gang kidnapped him and five others. They spent 311 days held by the Iraqi resistance group before being rescued by members of the US Army's Delta Force

programme.title: Dork Hunters from Outer Space
programme.desc: A group of heroes try to stop aliens taking over the universe

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: An artificial intelligence tries to erase the band from the internet, while Maxine asks X to spy on the girls, as she is worried one of them is thinking about quitting

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: X is kidnapped by a mysterious ninja. The girls seek the aid of their bodyguard's old karate teacher to learn the skills they will need to rescue him

programme.title: Zeek's Animal World
programme.desc: Follow alien robot Zeek as he tries to find out some fascinating facts about our animal kingdom

programme.title: UBOS
programme.desc: Magical animation following the adventures of three friends who discover a mysterious underworld at their boarding school

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Urobe takes charge of the Drakkar while Sven is sick - but ends up leaving all the important decisions to Ylva

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar makes fun of two dancing Irishmen, only to find himself challenged to a dance-off by their chief

programme.title: Dork Hunters from Outer Space
programme.desc: A group of heroes try to stop aliens taking over the universe

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef puts a vegetarian twist on a classic dish as she indulges in comfort food

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef celebrates the influence of Eastern cuisine in the capital by preparing sticky chicken with Malaysian salad, and soft-steamed buns

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen seeks a boyfriend for her socially inept elder sister Laura, so invites nervous horticulturalist Mike to a party. The pair discover they share a dislike of being fixed up, so depart together to prevent Helen objecting to them leaving. Bob Larbey's comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen and Phil are keen to know how things went between unlikely lovebirds Laura and Mike, so both end up invited to lunch - where they relate conflicting stories. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: Charlton Heston Presents the Bible
programme.desc: The renowned actor serves as storyteller an ancient Roman amphitheatre and other locales in Israel provide the settings, bringing the King James text to life

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams travels to the Wiltshire village home of Jim Stoodley, who at the age of 14 stole a US aircraft and took to the air with the intention of killing Adolf Hitler - earning himself a place in the Guinness Book of Records as the youngest pilot in history

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams investigates the ration-book fraudsters and coupon fiddlers spawned by the restrictions of the Second World War, and looks at the career of gangster Frankie Fraser, for whom the war provided an opportunity to get rich quick

programme.title: Assassin for Hire (1951)
programme.desc: An assassin who kills to finance his violinist brother's career is tricked into confessing his crimes by a wily police inspector. Drama, starring Sydney Tafler, Ronald Howard, Katherine Blake and John Hewer

programme.title: The Face at the Window (1939)
programme.desc: In 1880, Paris is terrorised by a hideous murderer. An innocent man is accused of being the killer by a respected aristocrat who longs to get him out of the way so he can seduce his fiancee - but may also have other reasons for wanting a scapegoat. Horror, starring Tod Slaughter and John Warwick

programme.title: Hot Enough for June (1964)
programme.desc: An unemployed writer gets a job for an industrialist and is sent on a business trip to Prague. However, he does not realise his boss is actually in the British Secret Service and is using him to unwittingly carry out an important mission - while his local chauffeur is really a Communist agent. Spy comedy, starring Dirk Bogarde, Sylva Koscina, Robert Morley, Leo McKern and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours for locals and tourists, exploring Elephant & Castle and Croydon. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Nigel Taylor, head of horticulture at the Royal Botanic Gardens, visits Paris to find out whether the gardens have been awarded the status of Unesco World Heritage Site. Back at Kew, the trees begin to suffer as staff deal with the worst drought to hit the site since records began

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: The effects of the drought begin to show, with one tree shedding a huge limb and others dropping their leaves at an alarming rate. Arboretum manager Ray Townsend is sent out to buy 35 hoses, hoping to press all the Gardens' irrigation points into service. Meanwhile, Tony Kirkham has an Italian job on his hands, locating a matching pair of mature trees some 1,000 miles away to add to one of Kew's oldest avenues, the Pagoda Vista

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: Queens
programme.desc: 16th-century Europe was a hotbed of tense international intrigue. Mary Stuart and Elizabeth I confront one another on matters religious, political and personal

programme.title: Closing the Ring (2007)
programme.desc: An old woman consumed by grief following the death of her husband receives a message from a stranger who claims to have found a ring belonging to her that was lost decades before. The discovery brings to light secrets from her past and causes her to look back to the beginnings of her marriage. Drama, starring Shirley MacLaine, Christopher Plummer and Neve Campbell

programme.title: Dying of the Light (2014)
programme.desc: A CIA agent is diagnosed with a degenerative condition that results in him being forced to retire from the world of intelligence. When he receives word that a terrorist who once tortured him has resurfaced, he goes rogue and sets out to assassinate the man while he is still able. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Anton Yelchin and Irene Jacob

programme.title: Helga, She Wolf of Stilberg (1978)
programme.desc: A sadistic female Nazi officer runs a women's prison during the Second World War, and forces the inmates to be her sexual playthings. However, the latest arrival, a determined resistance fighter, proves to be far more difficult to dominate. Prison drama, starring Malisa Longo and Patrizia Gori. In French

programme.title: The Crouches
programme.desc: Comedy series from Rab C Nesbitt creator Ian Pattison, about an extended black family living in Walworth, south-east London. In the first episode, teenager Adele's attempts to prove to her parents that businessman boyfriend Dennis is a respectable pillar of the community don't quite go to plan - until her brother Aiden gets into trouble with the police. Mona Hammond, Rudolph Walker and Robbie Gee star

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Soho and Milllwall, with fascinating facts for locals and tourists alike. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Fairy Tales for You
programme.desc: A series of animated fairy tales read by various celebrities

programme.title: Fairy Tales for You
programme.desc: An animated version of The Hare and the Tortoise read by Bobby Davro

programme.title: Fairy Tales for You
programme.desc: An animated version of Rumpelstiltskin, read by Bobby Davro

programme.title: Fairy Tales for You
programme.desc: An animated version of The Three Little Pigs read by Rik Mayall

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Nigel Taylor, head of horticulture at the Royal Botanic Gardens, visits Paris to find out whether the gardens have been awarded the status of Unesco World Heritage Site. Back at Kew, the trees begin to suffer as staff deal with the worst drought to hit the site since records began

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: The effects of the drought begin to show, with one tree shedding a huge limb and others dropping their leaves at an alarming rate. Arboretum manager Ray Townsend is sent out to buy 35 hoses, hoping to press all the Gardens' irrigation points into service. Meanwhile, Tony Kirkham has an Italian job on his hands, locating a matching pair of mature trees some 1,000 miles away to add to one of Kew's oldest avenues, the Pagoda Vista

programme.title: Supersonic Saucer (1956)
programme.desc: A group of children help protect a stranded alien from criminals who want to exploit his strange powers. Sci-fi adventure, starring Marcia Manolescue and Gillian Harrison

programme.title: Eagle Rock (1964)
programme.desc: A boy attempts to climb a mountain on his own, but when he gets into serious trouble, he needs the help of his friends. Family drama, starring Pip Rolls and Tony Paul

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: Flat Two ()
programme.desc: Based on the novel by Edgar Wallace. Two men visit a rich gambler on the night of his death, each with a strong motive for murder

programme.title: Kind Hearts and Coronets (1949)
programme.desc: The penniless grandson of a duke plots to eliminate the eight relatives standing between him and a vast inheritance. He shoots, drowns, poisons and explodes his way through his family tree, but his final triumph is ruined when his lady love's husband kills himself. Ealing black comedy, starring Alec Guinness as all eight potential victims, with Dennis Price, Valerie Hobson and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: Moby Dick (1956)
programme.desc: Obsessive skipper Captain Ahab leads his ship and crew on an epic quest to find and kill the legendary white whale that bit off his leg - but his lust for vengeance endangers all their lives. John Huston's seafaring adventure, based on Herman Melville's novel, starring Gregory Peck, Richard Baseheart, Orson Welles and Leo Genn

programme.title: This Could Go Anywhere
programme.desc: Phil Tufnell and Brendon McCullum travel all over New Zealand, playing golf and discovering the country's most famous and picturesque courses and dive into the unique delights of each region

programme.title: The Crouches
programme.desc: Comedy series from Rab C Nesbitt creator Ian Pattison, about an extended black family living in Walworth, south-east London. In the first episode, teenager Adele's attempts to prove to her parents that businessman boyfriend Dennis is a respectable pillar of the community don't quite go to plan - until her brother Aiden gets into trouble with the police. Mona Hammond, Rudolph Walker and Robbie Gee star

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: A drunk John Hurt arrives in the neighbourhood, while Joe takes advantage of Michael's absence by taking over his house. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Sex Ed (2014)
programme.desc: A teacher arrives at a new school, and is appalled to discover his teenage students are receiving no sex education. He takes it upon himself to provide the facts of life, despite objection from the local preacher. However, his plan is complicated by his still being a virgin and falling in love with a pupil's older sister. Comedy, starring Haley Joel Osment and Lorenza Izzo

programme.title: The Occupation
programme.desc: The incredible true story of Sara Góralnik who at age 13 survived the Holocaust by taking refuge in a small Ukrainian village and passing as a Christian after her entire family was killed by Nazis

programme.title: Emmanuelle in Soho (1981)
programme.desc: A woman seeking fame and fortune in Soho plays a pivotal role in the struggling marriage of a photographer and his wife, and conspires with them on a plot to blackmail a pornography producer. Erotic drama, starring Angie Quick and Julie Lee

programme.title: Queen of the Blues (1979)
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a failing Soho nightclub and hope to turn its fortunes around - but they end up being threatened by gangsters demanding protection money. Fortunately, salvation is at hand in an enthusiastic stripper, who comes up with a plan to save the day. Drama, starring Mary Millington and Rosemary England

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: Shaw Somers, Lucy Jones and Dr Giovanni Miletto give advice to a 42-year-old woman who has been eliminating entire food groups from her diet in an effort to combat her traumatising fish odour syndrome. They also help a teenager who worries severe eczema will prevent him fulfilling his dream of becoming an aeroplane cabin crew member. Three hard-drinking students from Leeds put hangover cures to the test, and Dr Pixie McKenna investigates the veracity of health claims made on food packaging

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between 2-8 miles from around Britain. Andrew White will guide you through the best walkways that Great Britain has to offer.

programme.title: Inside Claridge's
programme.desc: For the first time in its long history, the famously discreet luxury hotel in Mayfair, central London, opens its doors to documentary cameras, with director Jane Treays spending a year behind the scenes following staff and guests. The Melchors from California arrive for their annual two-week stay, rekindling their relationship with personal butler Michael Lynch, while energetic general manager Thomas Kochs travels to New York to drum up new business from leading US travel agents

programme.title: Eagle Rock (1964)
programme.desc: A boy attempts to climb a mountain on his own, but when he gets into serious trouble, he needs the help of his friends. Family drama, starring Pip Rolls and Tony Paul

programme.title: Angels One Five (1953)
programme.desc: A new recruit joins a squadron of British fighter pilots in the summer of 1940, as they prepare to face a confrontation with the overwhelming forces of the Luftwaffe over the English Channel, while the ground crew at their base anxiously awaits reports from the conflict. Second World War adventure, starring Jack Hawkins and John Gregson

programme.title: I Was a Spy (1933)
programme.desc: A Belgian nurse infiltrates German High Command to supply the British with vital information during the First World War One. Fact-based spy thriller, starring Madeleine Carroll, Conrad Veidt, Donald Calthorp and Nigel Bruce

programme.title: Assassin for Hire (1951)
programme.desc: An assassin who kills to finance his violinist brother's career is tricked into confessing his crimes by a wily police inspector. Drama, starring Sydney Tafler, Ronald Howard, Katherine Blake and John Hewer

programme.title: Dear Murderer (1947)
programme.desc: A man believes he has committed the perfect crime when he kills his wife's two lovers in a jealous rage. Melodrama, starring Eric Portman, Dennis Price, Jack Warner, Greta Gynt and Hazel Court

programme.title: The Face at the Window (1939)
programme.desc: In 1880, Paris is terrorised by a hideous murderer. An innocent man is accused of being the killer by a respected aristocrat who longs to get him out of the way so he can seduce his fiancee - but may also have other reasons for wanting a scapegoat. Horror, starring Tod Slaughter and John Warwick

programme.title: Haunters of the Deep (1984)
programme.desc: An American businessman tries to reopen a Cornish tin mine, but the ghosts that haunt the tunnels have other ideas. Supernatural drama, starring Andrew Keir and Barbara Ewing

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Charlton Heston Presents the Bible
programme.desc: The renowned actor serves as storyteller an ancient Roman amphitheatre and other locales in Israel provide the settings, bringing the King James text to life

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike and Laura agree to go on a date, but chaos ensues in the restaurant when Laura loses a contact lens. Starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Laura buys a new dress for a special dinner with Ben. However, when her date reveals he is married, Mike happens by to pick up the pieces with his usual charm. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics

programme.title: Golden Years (2016)
programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Closing the Ring (2007)
programme.desc: An old woman consumed by grief following the death of her husband receives a message from a stranger who claims to have found a ring belonging to her that was lost decades before. The discovery brings to light secrets from her past and causes her to look back to the beginnings of her marriage. Drama, starring Shirley MacLaine, Christopher Plummer and Neve Campbell

programme.title: Sex Ed (2014)
programme.desc: A teacher arrives at a new school, and is appalled to discover his teenage students are receiving no sex education. He takes it upon himself to provide the facts of life, despite objection from the local preacher. However, his plan is complicated by his still being a virgin and falling in love with a pupil's older sister. Comedy, starring Haley Joel Osment and Lorenza Izzo

programme.title: Hallows Eve (2016)
programme.desc: Shadowy figures in masks begin appearing around an estate with a reputation for street crime, and leave behind graffiti warning of their intent to take over the area on Halloween. The members of a street gang vow to take a stand against the invaders, but are unprepared for the shocking truth behind their enemies' masks. Horror, starring Sarah Akokhia and Ethan Taylor

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Bankside and East Dulwich for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Inside Claridge's
programme.desc: For the first time in its long history, the famously discreet luxury hotel in Mayfair, central London, opens its doors to documentary cameras, with director Jane Treays spending a year behind the scenes following staff and guests. The Melchors from California arrive for their annual two-week stay, rekindling their relationship with personal butler Michael Lynch, while energetic general manager Thomas Kochs travels to New York to drum up new business from leading US travel agents

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Watford. Action from the 2012/13 Championship play-off final at Wembley Stadium, as the teams contested a place in the top flight alongside the already promoted Cardiff City and Hull City. Palace reached this match when Wilfried Zaha scored a brace in a 2-0 away win against Brighton & Hove Albion after a goalless draw in the first leg. Meanwhile, Watford bounced back from narrowly missing out on automatic promotion by overcoming Leicester City in dramatic fashion, with Troy Deeney scoring an injury time-winner on the counter after Manuel Almunia had saved a penalty

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Watford. Action from the 2012/13 Championship play-off final at Wembley Stadium, as the teams contested a place in the top flight alongside the already promoted Cardiff City and Hull City. Palace reached this match when Wilfried Zaha scored a brace in a 2-0 away win against Brighton & Hove Albion after a goalless draw in the first leg. Meanwhile, Watford bounced back from narrowly missing out on automatic promotion by overcoming Leicester City in dramatic fashion, with Troy Deeney scoring an injury time-winner on the counter after Manuel Almunia had saved a penalty

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Brentford v Yeovil Town. Action from the 2012/13 League One play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where the remaining place in the following season's Championship was decided. Brentford agonisingly missed out on automatic promotion on the final day of the regular campaign, but recovered to defeat Swindon Town on penalties in their semi-final, while Yeovil were 2-1 aggregate victors over Sheffield United

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Northampton Town. Action from the 2012/13 League Two play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where promotion to League One awaited the winners. The Bantams were making their second appearance of the season at Wembley having also reached the League Cup final following an outstanding giant-killing run, and they needed to come from behind to edge out Burton Albion to reach this stage. The Cobblers ended a run of recent poor form to win 1-0 in both legs of their semi-final against Cheltenham Town to move within one match of reaching the third tier of English football
